repositories.brief_summary: Ephemeron GC fixes landed, compiler profiling overhead eliminated, Windows Unix.select bug fixed, and Antonin Décimo added as maintainer.
repositories.new_features_summary: Compiler profiling skip when unused (30% faster microbench), Windows Unix.select fd drop fixed, Char.escaped docs corrected.
repositories.new_features:
- **Compiler profiling overhead eliminated** when `-dprofile`/`-dcounters`/`-dtimings` are not in use: the backend previously called `Profile.record_call` once per function per compilation phase regardless of whether profiling was enabled, causing up to 30% overhead in microbenchmarks with many small functions. This fix skips all profiling work unless it is requested. As part of this change, the `profile_column` type was moved from `Profile` to `Clflags`, reflected in updated `utils/clflags.mli` and `utils/profile.mli` signatures. ([#14832](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14832))
- Fixed a bug in the **Win32 `Unix.select` implementation** where `socket_poll_add` returned the dispatched node rather than the (potentially updated) list head, causing some file descriptors to be silently dropped from `Unix.select` results and a memory leak. A regression test was also added. ([#14764](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14764))
- Corrected the **`Char.escaped` documentation** in `stdlib/char.mli`: the previous text incorrectly stated that double-quote characters are escaped, when in fact `Char.escaped '"'` returns `"\""` — double-quotes are not escaped by this function. ([#14836](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14836))
- Two **ephemeron GC correctness fixes** merged: [#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734) fixes ephemeron orphaning during `Phase_sweep_ephe` (a case that could leave ephemerons with dangling pointers across GC cycles), and [#14733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14733) clarifies and corrects the semantics of per-domain ephemeron work counters, removing a pessimistic `ephe_next_round` call that could cause unnecessary repeated work in ephemeron-heavy multicore benchmarks. ([#14733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14733), [#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734))
- Added a **regression test for warning 60** (unused module) location bug: warning 60 was previously reported at the location of the wrong file when a non-aliasable module substitution was involved, confusing users compiling with build systems. ([commit 1d2d55d](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/commit/1d2d55d2f0c30d12e907c0d200e3a4f29f643f5f), [#12745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12745))
- **Manual option documentation** improved: `-match-context-rows` now shows its `number` argument in the options listing, several formatting and wording fixes across `unified-options.etex` and the man pages for `ocamlc` and `ocamlopt`. ([#14838](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14838))
repositories.activity_summary: Newly opened PR targets runtime events hang fix; ongoing GC pacing, extensible IO, faster minor GC, and constructor eta-expansion proposals continue.
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened**: [Michael Bacarella](https://github.com/mbacarella) opened [#14834](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14834) to fix a hang in runtime events when an event exceeds the ring buffer size (originally reported in [#13335](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13335)), proposing to terminate with a fatal error rather than loop indefinitely; the PR is under review with [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) suggesting minor cleanups.
- **New maintainer**: [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) was officially added to `CONTRIBUTING.md` as a maintainer, following a maintainer meeting decision ([#14837](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14837)).
- **CI improvements**: [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) merged [#14821](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14821) to disable "cold" tests in GitHub Actions and AppVeyor CI (as permitted by [#14713](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14713)); [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) hardened the C++ test to skip correctly when building for musl libc targets ([#14827](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14827)).
- **Cross-compilation build fix**: [@shym](https://github.com/shym) merged [#14835](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14835) to fix a broken `.depend` file caused by `ocamldep` misidentifying `Str` as the `otherlibs/str` library instead of `Ast_helper.Str` in the new `Typing_recovery_state` module (introduced in [#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241)), which had broken cross-compilation CI since that merge.
- **Build cleanup**: The fake `ocamlyacc.opt` file produced during cross-compilation builds was removed from the build system ([#14833](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14833)).
- **Changelog housekeeping**: [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) added a missing PR number ([#14525](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14525)) to the `Fun.todo` changelog entry ([#14829](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14829)); [Enea Zani](https://github.com/Frenxys) fixed minor grammar issues across three documentation files ([#14830](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14830)).
- **Ongoing — GC pacing**: [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) continues work on [#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796) (new GC-pacing logic), with [Eduardo Souza](https://github.com/udesou) providing comprehensive macro-benchmark results showing wall-time improvements across all tested `(s, o)` parameter combinations, though at some RSS cost.
- **Ongoing — ephemeron correctness**: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) has three further ephemeron PRs awaiting review: ensuring no orphaned ephemerons remain across GC cycles ([#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813)), waiting for all domains to finish marking before starting a new ephemeron round ([#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774)), and a test suite with repro cases for known ephemeron bugs ([#14745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14745)).
- **Ongoing — faster minor GC**: [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) updated [#14571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14571) after addressing review comments, offering 2-3% total runtime improvements in promotion-heavy workloads by inlining the hot path in `oldify_one`.
- **Ongoing — language feature proposals**: [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) opened [#14803](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14803) proposing to allow constructors to be used directly as builder functions (auto eta-expansion in function-type contexts, e.g. `List.map Some [1;2;3]`); [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) has a draft [#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775) implementing extensible IO channels per the modular IO RFC.
- **Ongoing — type system**: [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) continues [#14746](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14746) to allow alias following when checking signature inclusion, with review from [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) identifying remaining issues.
- **Ongoing — C API**: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) is working on [#14804](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14804) to expose `caml_ba_compare` and related bigarray functions outside of `CAML_INTERNALS`, reducing the need for external libraries to define that macro.
- **Ongoing — libunix fixes**: [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) has [#14740](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14740) with several small Unix library correctness fixes including missing blocking sections in `realpath` and `lockf`.
- **Ongoing — OCAMLRUNPARAM**: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) opened [#14822](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14822) adding tests for current OCAMLRUNPARAM parsing behavior (including quirks like `b` being equivalent to `b=1`), as groundwork for follow-up cleanups.
repositories.discussions_summary: Cross-compilation maturity for Android targets discussed, with users asking about `make crossopt` status.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [Discussion #14451](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/discussions/14451) on **cross-compilation status** was active this week: [@yotambarnoy](https://github.com/yotambarnoy) asked about the maturity of cross-compiling OCaml for Android using `make crossopt`, noting they were encountering errors. This coincided with ongoing cross-compilation build fixes in [#14835](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14835) and [#14808](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14808).
repositories.trends_summary: Sustained focus on multicore GC correctness (especially ephemerons), incremental compiler performance, and cross-compilation robustness.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Ephemeron correctness as a sustained effort**: The merging of [#14733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14733) and [#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734) continues a multi-week series of GC fixes led by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), with three more ephemeron PRs ([#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813), [#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774), [#14745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14745)) in the review pipeline, reflecting the complexity of getting multicore ephemeron semantics correct.
- **Compiler performance improvements gaining attention**: Both [#14832](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14832) (profiling overhead) and the ongoing [#14571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14571) (minor GC) and [#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796) (GC pacing) represent a pattern of targeted, measurable performance improvements with benchmark data backing them.
- **Cross-compilation robustness**: Multiple PRs this week ([#14835](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14835), [#14833](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14833), previously [#14808](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14808)) address cross-compilation edge cases, and the active discussion [#14451](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/discussions/14451) shows user interest in Android/musl targets.
- **OxCaml upstream flow**: Several PRs this week (e.g. [#14832](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14832) from oxcaml/oxcaml#6141, [#14571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14571) upstreamed from OxCaml) continue the pattern of performance and correctness work flowing from the OxCaml fork back into upstream OCaml.

repositories.brief_summary: HTTP repository reftest merged; new PRs queued for tar library integration and conditional opam root upgrades.
repositories.new_features_summary: New `http-server` reftest command and comprehensive HTTP repository test suite added to the testing engine.
repositories.new_features:
- New **`http-server` command** added to the reftest engine, launching a minimal HTTP server accessible via `http://localhost:$HTTPSERVERPORT` serving `$BASEDIR`, with the port stored in `HTTPSERVERPORT` — enabling HTTP-based repository testing in the reftest framework ([#6939](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6939))
- New **`repository-http.test`** reftest covering the full HTTP repository backend lifecycle: initial repository checkout, package addition, package removal, and package update operations, with debug logging and sed-based output normalization ([#6939](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6939))
- **`micro_httpd`** added to CI test scripts as the HTTP server dependency for the new HTTP repository tests ([#6939](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6939))
repositories.activity_summary: Several PRs ready to merge advance the tar library integration and opam root upgrade mechanism, part of the ongoing HTTP repository performance initiative.
repositories.activity:
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) opened [#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945) (**Use `tar` library instead of `tar` tool**), introducing a new `OpamTar` module with functions for creating and manipulating tar.gz archives natively — marked READY TO MERGE and queued as a prerequisite for [#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) opened [#6949](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6949) (**conditional hard upgrade for opam root**), adding a mechanism to perform hard upgrades only when necessary so most users are unaffected — marked READY TO MERGE and part of the [#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625) story
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) opened [#6947](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6947) fixing an **`undefined variable` error** when a lock file filter references an undefined variable, failing gracefully in strict mode and defaulting to false otherwise (fixing [#6946](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6946))
- [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) has [#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938) open adding **`prefix` and `prefixexec` sections to `.install` files**, addressing [#6919](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6919)
- [#6939](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6939) (**Add HTTP repository reftest**) was **merged** by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou), completing the first test infrastructure piece for the HTTP repository backend rework
- The long-running draft [#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625) (**improve performance of `opam update/init`** by using tar.gz repositories as-is) continues to accumulate queued PRs, now including [#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945) and [#6949](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6949)
repositories.discussions_summary: Review of #6945 raised API design questions on type naming, I/O strategy, and directory metadata preservation in the new OpamTar module.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) provided detailed review on [#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945), raising questions about **type naming** (`archive`/`archived_file` vs current names), whether to use a `to_fd` function instead of buffering via `to_buffer`, and the fact that **directory metadata and empty directories are silently dropped** when modifying an existing archive — suggesting these limitations be documented in the module
- [R. Boujbou](https://github.com/rjbou) referenced [#6948](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6948) in a review comment on [#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945) after [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) identified that the `OpamFilename.starts_with` function used internally performs a substring check rather than a proper path prefix check — a subtle correctness bug also relevant to [#6940](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6940)
- [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) approved [#6947](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6947) with a simple "Looks good to me" after reviewing the lock file undefined variable fix
repositories.trends_summary: Development is focused on replacing external tool dependencies with native OCaml library implementations as part of the HTTP repository performance overhaul.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **`tar` tool replacement** effort is advancing steadily: [#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945) introduces a native `OpamTar` OCaml module, feeding into [#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)'s goal of eliminating the external `tar` command dependency and enabling in-process archive manipulation
- **Test infrastructure** for the repository backend is being built out alongside the feature work, with [#6939](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6939) establishing HTTP repository reftests and the new `http-server` reftest primitive, making future HTTP backend changes easier to validate
- **Bug surfacing from code review**: the review process on [#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945) uncovered [#6948](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6948) (`OpamFilename.starts_with` incorrect substring semantics), which is also linked to the Windows path separator issue [#6940](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6940) — reflecting how the concentrated repository rework is exposing latent correctness issues
repositories.contributors_summary: R. Boujbel drove most activity this week with merged tests, new PRs, and issue reports; Nathan Rebours provided code reviews.
repositories.contributors:
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) had [#6939](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6939) merged (HTTP repository reftest), opened [#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945) (tar library integration), [#6949](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6949) (conditional hard upgrade), and [#6947](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6947) (lock file undefined variable fix), and filed issues [#6948](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6948) (`OpamFilename.starts_with` bug) and [#6950](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6950) (performance follow-up for `OpamTar.Inplace.write`)
- [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) reviewed [#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945) with detailed feedback on API design and documentation gaps, and approved [#6947](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6947)
- [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) has [#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938) open adding `prefix` and `prefixexec` sections to `.install` files
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) maintains the overarching [#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625) draft PR, which received a review comment from [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) this week
- [Nicolas Berthier](https://github.com/nberth) filed [#6940](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6940) reporting Windows directory separator handling failures in `opam install dir/*.opam`, which received attention this week as it intersects with the `starts_with` bug

repositories.brief_summary: New PR opened to add hover information for OCaml warnings, addressing a long-standing feature request.
repositories.activity_summary: One new PR opened this week adding hover support for OCaml warnings, plus an ongoing automated Nix flake lock update.
repositories.activity:
- [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) opened [#1606](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1606) **"Hover on warnings"**, adding 216 lines to implement hover information display for OCaml compiler warnings, addressing the feature request tracked in [#717](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/717); [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) commented requesting unit tests for the core function before merging

repositories.discussions_summary: Review discussion on PR #1606 focused on the need for unit tests for the warning hover implementation.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) raised a review concern on [#1606](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1606), noting the hover parsing function is complex and requesting **unit tests** be added before the PR can proceed toward merging
repositories.trends_summary: Continued work on enriching LSP hover responses with more OCaml-specific semantic information.
repositories.emerging_trends: - The "Hover on warnings" PR [#1606](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1606) continues a pattern of improving **hover response richness** in ocaml-lsp, making compiler diagnostic information more accessible directly in editors without requiring users to look up warning documentation externally

repositories.contributors:

repositories.brief_summary: Signature-help type alias fix merged, old issues closed with tests, and Emacs 31 compatibility update opened.
repositories.new_features_summary: Signature-help now expands type aliases to correctly display function signatures through type aliases.
repositories.new_features:
- Fixed **signature-help with type aliases** so that values declared with a type alias like `type t = int -> unit` are now correctly shown as functions in signature help ([#2067](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2067), fixes [#1927](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1927)) by [Carine Morel](https://github.com/lyrm) — `Ctype.expand_head` is now applied to expand type aliases before checking for arrow types, and mid-call aliases are also expanded when more arguments are supplied than visible arrows allow
- Added **test coverage for polymorphic variant completion** confirming that [#298](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/298) (reported in 2014) is now working, closing the long-standing issue ([#2075](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2075)) by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd)
repositories.activity_summary: Locate fix for let-punnings is under active review; several long-standing issues closed via test additions and cleanup.
repositories.activity:
- **Locate fix for let-punnings** ([#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066)) by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) remains open and under review — the PR introduces a tie-breaking mechanism in `Mbrowse` to disambiguate overlapping node locations (pattern vs expression) for queries like `locate` and `type-enclosing`, fixing [#1796](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1796); reviewers [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) and [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) are discussing the final shape of the variant types used
- **Emacs 31 compatibility** update ([#2076](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2076)) by [Danny Willems](https://github.com/dannywillems) opened during the week to replace the obsolete `when-let` macro with `let` + `when` in `merlin--construct-point`, silencing a byte-compiler warning in Emacs 31 while preserving compatibility with Emacs 25.1+
- **`location_trie` field removal** in `src/ocaml-index/lib/index.ml` and related files ([#2071](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2071)) by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon), cleaning up a field unused since 2022
- **Test suite hardening** around the `occurrences` query: [#2073](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2073) by [Carine Morel](https://github.com/lyrm) removed the FIXME marker in `tests/test-dirs/occurrences/issue1404.t` and added two regression tests for cursor-position disambiguation, confirming [#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1404) is fully resolved
- **Get description of warnings** PR ([#2074](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2074)) by [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) was opened and then self-closed after determining the functionality is better served through `ocamllsp` directly, which already exposes warning descriptions
- A batch of **stale issues** were triaged and closed, including [#369](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/369), [#61](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/61), [#298](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/298), [#598](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/598), [#425](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/425), [#453](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/453), [#1191](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1191), [#1311](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1311), [#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1404), [#1900](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1900), and [#1993](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1993), many dating back to 2013–2021
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on the locate/let-punning PR about tie-breaking for overlapping AST node locations and polymorphic vs regular variants.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- On [#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066), [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) raised a regression where the initial fix broke `type-enclosing` on punned values (the pattern type should be displayed, not the expression type), leading [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) to revise the approach with a general tie-breaking function that can select pattern vs expression nodes depending on query type
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) questioned whether polymorphic variants were necessary in the tie-breaker type definition in [#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066); [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) agreed to switch to regular variants
- [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) self-closed [#2074](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2074) after concluding that warning description retrieval is already accessible via `ocamllsp`, avoiding duplication of functionality
repositories.trends_summary: Ongoing effort to close old issues by verifying fixes with tests, and active refinement of the locate/occurrences query infrastructure.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- A recurring pattern this week: long-standing issues (some from 2013–2021) are being triaged and closed by verifying fixes are already in place and adding regression tests to pin the behaviour ([#2073](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2073), [#2075](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2075), and the batch closures of [#298](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/298), [#369](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/369), [#598](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/598), [#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1404))
- The `locate` and `occurrences` query infrastructure is receiving attention around **AST node disambiguation** for overlapping locations ([#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066)), reflecting an effort to improve correctness of cursor-position resolution in complex syntax like let-punnings and labelled arguments
repositories.contributors_summary: Five contributors were active across code, reviews, and issue triage.
repositories.contributors:

- [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) opened the locate/let-punning fix ([#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066), still under review) and merged the polymorphic variant completion test ([#2075](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2075))
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) removed the long-unused `location_trie` field ([#2071](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2071))

- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) reviewed [#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066), [#2071](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2071), [#2073](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2073), and [#2075](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2075), and performed triage to close multiple stale issues

repositories.brief_summary: Active week with DragonFly BSD support, anonymous actions expansion, Melange single-context work, and extensive watch-mode and RPC improvements.
repositories.new_features_summary: DragonFly BSD support was added and the `dune pkg` solver now ignores opam upper bounds on dune itself.
repositories.new_features:
- DragonFly BSD is now a recognised platform in dune, fixing file-watcher and OS detection paths; merged in [#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14774) by [Michael Neumann](https://github.com/mneumann).
- `dune pkg` now strips upper-bound version constraints on `dune` itself from opam `depends` formulas before solving, so packages with stale `< 3.17` style bounds no longer cause hard solver failures ([#14638](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14638) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter), closes [#14637](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14637)).
- `melange.ppx_runtime_libraries` field added to library stanzas for ppx rewriters that need separate native and Melange runtime libraries ([#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14796) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro)).
- Shell command completion support opened as [#14779](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14779) by [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS), leveraging the newly vendored cmdliner 2.1.1 to expose bash, zsh, and PowerShell sub-command and flag completion.
repositories.activity_summary: Ongoing work centred on expanding anonymous actions to eliminate intermediate files, hardening the watch-mode scheduler, and advancing the single-context-universal Melange installation stack.
repositories.activity:
- [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) continued the `single-context-universal` Melange stack: installation rules ([#14800](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14800)), `melange.ppx_runtime_libraries` ([#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14796)), install-conditional-libraries tests ([#14812](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14812)), preprocess-melange-only-modules ([#14814](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14814)), and a cross-compilation target-env fix ([#14817](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14817)).
- [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) expanded anonymous actions to cover pkg-config queries ([#14782](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14782)), mdx dependency queries ([#14787](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14787)), inline-test partitions ([#14788](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14788)), and odoc dependency queries ([#14786](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14786), still open).
- Scheduler file-watcher plumbing was refactored in [#14778](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14778): watcher events now flow through a thread-safe fiber channel, simplifying the event queue to handle only memo invalidations.
- `FSEvents` watcher startup and failure propagation were fixed in [#14764](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14764) and root-path handling in the `Watch_trie` corrected in [#14785](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14785).
- Thread pool worker spawning was fixed to create new workers when queued work exceeds idle capacity ([#14707](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14707)).
- The vendored cmdliner was updated to 2.1.1 ([#14636](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12515), [#14636 PR](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14636)), enabling shell completion and updated API.
- Cache copy-mode entries now have their ctime refreshed on restore so the trim logic retains recently-used entries ([#14777](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14777)).
- Build path separator inconsistency on Windows fixed: `Build.to_string` now uses `External.append_local` ([#14616](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14616)).
- [Danny Willems](https://github.com/dannywillems) opened [#14798](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14798) fixing Emacs byte-compile warnings in `editor-integration/emacs` and adding an Emacs CI workflow.
- [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) opened the per-module narrowing Melange layer [#14732](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14732) (draft) as part of the larger [#14492](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14492) stack.
- [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) opened [#14810](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14810) proposing a `%{git-sha}` rule variable following the discussion in [#14723](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14723).
repositories.discussions_summary: The 3.24.0 release is progressing with blockers under resolution; a `buggy enabled_if for executable` regression and cache poisoning with anonymous actions drew attention.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- The 3.24.0 release tracker [#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14620) remained open; the opam `mdx` with-test breakage ([#14724](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14724)) was identified as a behavioural change rather than a regression and closed.
- [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) filed [#14789](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14789) reporting that `enabled_if` in `(executable ...)` incorrectly rejects `%{read:...}` variables even when the dune language is at 3.23.
- [hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) reported intermittent missing `.d` dependency files after the ocamldep-to-anonymous-actions migration ([#14767](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14767)), suspected to be a cache poisoning issue.
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) reported a 3.21 → 3.23 regression breaking `ocaml-re` tests with an `modules_and_obj_dir: failed lookup` internal error ([#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14775)), under investigation.
- Sandbox-breaking of `git rev-parse` rules ([#14723](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14723)) prompted discussion about exposing a plain `git sha` in dune, leading [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) to open [#14810](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14810).
repositories.trends_summary: The project is systematically replacing file-backed intermediate results with anonymous actions and advancing first-class single-context support for Melange.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Anonymous actions are being adopted broadly across the build system (pkg-config, mdx, odoc, inline-test partitions, C compiler detection) to eliminate temporary files and simplify the rule graph.
- The `single-context-universal` track ([#10507](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/10507)) is advancing steadily, with installation, conditional libraries, and cross-compilation target-env correctness all landing this week.
- Cross-compilation is receiving attention with both test coverage and bug fixes for configurator environment isolation ([#14818](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14818), [#14817](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14817)).
- BSD platform coverage continues to expand; DragonFly BSD follows the earlier NetBSD support.
repositories.issues_summary: Open issues include an enabled_if regression with %{read:...} in executables, cache corruption producing null-byte module names, and a 3.23 regression in ocaml-re test builds.
repositories.good_first_issues:
- [Print a separator when `--error-reporting=twice` is used](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14757): a self-contained UX enhancement to add a visible header between the normal output and the repeated error summary.
- [`dune show targets` should hide source files](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13779): filtering source files from `dune show targets` output by default, with a flag to expose them.
repositories.contributors_summary: Rudi Grinberg and Antonio Nuno Monteiro led commit volume, with Michael Neumann, Ali Caglayan, Ambre Austen Suhamy, Robin Bate Boerop, and Danny Willems also contributing.
repositories.contributors:
- [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) — anonymous action expansion, scheduler refactoring, cache improvements, RPC cleanup, and watcher fixes.
- [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) — single-context-universal Melange installation stack and cross-compilation fixes.
- [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) — `dune pkg` upper-bound stripping and issue triage.
- [Michael Neumann](https://github.com/mneumann) — DragonFly BSD platform support.
- [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS) — shell command completion PR.
- [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) — per-module narrowing stack (Melange layer, performance layers).
- [Danny Willems](https://github.com/dannywillems) — Emacs integration byte-compile warning fixes and CI workflow.
- [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) — `%{git-sha}` proposal and sandbox workaround discussion.

repositories.brief_summary: OxCaml support PRs under active review; OxCaml layouts issue closed; new sherlodoc serve bug reported.
repositories.activity_summary: New PRs for raise tag rendering and sherlodoc web UI; ongoing OxCaml modalities and zero-alloc annotation work under review.
repositories.activity:
- **Inline raise tag rendering** fix opened by [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d) in [#1440](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1440) to address issues where `@raise` tag content renders as block-level instead of inline in HTML output, aiming to fix [#1136](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1136) and [#1198](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1198); [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) noted the fix should be moved upstream into the Document layer to cover all backends
- **`sherlodoc serve` web UI** broken due to missing `www` directory reported by [mt_caret](https://github.com/mt-caret) in [#1441](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1441); the `www/` directory was not migrated when sherlodoc was moved from its original repo, causing the server to always report `Webserver unavailable`; a branch with the fix exists and a PR is anticipated
- **OxCaml modalities** PR [#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420) by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) remains under review with [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) providing detailed feedback on modality loading from `.cmt` files, cppo usage patterns, and test coverage; this PR fixes [#1416](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1416)
- **Zero-alloc annotation for OxCaml** PR [#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422) by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) is open, adding support for retaining `[@@zero_alloc]` annotations from source through to parsed output
- **Multiline link fix** PR [#1439](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1439) by [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d) addresses issue [#865](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/865) for multi-line link reformatting; review from [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) identified ordering issues with `trim` and `multilines_link_to_link` calls
- **OxCaml layouts** tracking issue [#1415](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1415) was closed on 2026-05-27, indicating that layout annotation support was merged via [#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410); the broader OxCaml tracking issue [#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411) was updated to reflect remaining work
repositories.discussions_summary: Active code review on OxCaml modalities PR with discussion on cppo usage, cmt loading behavior, and test completeness.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) posted multiple detailed review comments on [#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420) questioning whether modalities are intentionally skipped when loading `.cmt` files, suggesting refactoring cppo guards into dedicated helper functions (`read_value_modalities`, `read_field_modalities`), and requesting additional test cases for GADTs, inline records with modalities, and `val` declarations with modality annotations
- [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) reviewed [#1439](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1439) and flagged an incorrect ordering of `String.trim` and `multilines_link_to_link` transformations, as well as a missing CHANGES entry for the fix
- [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) recommended in [#1440](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1440) that the raise tag inlining fix should be implemented in the `Document` layer rather than the HTML backend alone, to avoid backend-specific workarounds and broken multi-paragraph raise tags
repositories.trends_summary: Sustained OxCaml feature expansion with new external contributors joining the effort.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **OxCaml feature parity** work continues with multiple concurrent PRs ([#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420), [#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)) targeting modalities and zero-alloc annotations, building on the recently merged layout support ([#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410))
- **New external contributors** [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d) and [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) are actively submitting first-time PRs addressing both OxCaml features and existing bugs, broadening community involvement
- **sherlodoc integration** is surfacing post-migration issues ([#1441](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1441)), suggesting the web UI component needs attention following the move from the standalone sherlodoc repository
repositories.issues_summary: One long-standing good first issue remains open for rendering comments on polymorphic variant inherits.
repositories.good_first_issues: - [**Comments on polymorphic variant inherit not rendered**](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/846): reported by [Jules Aguillon](https://github.com/Julow) ([#846](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/846)), this bug involves documentation comments attached to `| bar (** bar *)` entries in polymorphic variant type definitions not appearing in the rendered output; labelled `bug`, `output`, and `good-first-issue`
repositories.contributors_summary: Tim ats opened two PRs, Marek Kubica advanced OxCaml zero-alloc work, and Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac reviewed three open PRs.
repositories.contributors:
- [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d) opened two PRs this week: [#1439](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1439) fixing multiline link handling (issue [#865](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/865)) and [#1440](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1440) inlining raise tag content in HTML rendering (issues [#1136](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1136), [#1198](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1198)), both receiving review feedback from the core team

- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) maintains [#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420) for OxCaml modalities support, incorporating ongoing review feedback
- [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) provided code review across three PRs ([#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420), [#1439](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1439), [#1440](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1440)) with detailed technical guidance on implementation approach, test coverage, and architectural decisions
- [mt_caret](https://github.com/mt-caret) filed issue [#1441](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1441) reporting the broken `sherlodoc serve` web UI and provided a branch with a potential fix
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) updated the OxCaml tracking issue [#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411) to reflect the closure of [#1415](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1415)

repositories.brief_summary: Platform release changelogs published for nine tools including OCaml 5.5.0~beta1, odoc 3.2.0, and Dune 3.24.0~alpha0 scraped.
repositories.new_features_summary: Release announcements published for OCaml 5.5.0~beta1, odoc 3.2.0, ocamlformat 0.29.0, merlin, and more.
repositories.new_features:
- **Platform release changelogs** for nine tools moved from draft to published status on ocaml.org, giving the community official release notes for dune-release 2.2.1, mdx 2.5.2, merlin v5.7-505~preview, merlin v5.7.1-504, ocaml-lsp 1.26.0-5.5~preview, OCaml 5.5.0~beta1, ocamlformat 0.29.0, odoc 3.2.0, opam-publish 3.0.0, and utop 2.17.0 ([#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641))
- **Dune 3.24.0~alpha0 release page** scraped automatically, documenting fixes for promotion failures, `dune build @doc` picking up odoc without extra config, directory target promotion to stable, and the new `%{pkg:<package>:<section>:<path>}` pform ([#3666](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3666))
- **OCaml Planet** updated with Anil's `.plan` post covering Pint of Science, OxCaml dissertations, and TESSERA 1.1, plus OCaml Weekly News for 26 May 2026 ([#3663](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3663), [#3665](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3665))
repositories.activity_summary: Backstage OCaml wasm/wasi prototype post opened; platform release changelogs merged; docs feedback raised on Core library usage.
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened**: [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) opened [#3667](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3667) adding a "Backstage OCaml" entry about the wasm-of-ocaml WASI prototype, still awaiting review
- **Newly opened issue**: [opale](https://github.com/N1ark) raised [#3664](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3664) questioning the use of the `Core` library in GC documentation examples, arguing that docs should demonstrate stdlib-only patterns to help surface ergonomic gaps
- **Platform release changelogs** merged: [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) submitted [#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641) publishing release announcements covering nine platform tools that had shipped over the preceding months without entries on ocaml.org; the PR was closed without merge on its older automated sibling [#3650](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3650) in favour of the newer scrape [#3666](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3666)
repositories.discussions_summary: Feedback opened on Core library use in GC documentation, advocating for stdlib-only examples.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [opale](https://github.com/N1ark) opened [#3664](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3664) noting that the garbage collector documentation uses `Core` library functions (e.g. `Gc.tune`) that do not exist in stdlib, and that the stdlib equivalent requires more verbose code; the issue frames this as an opportunity to highlight ergonomic gaps in stdlib rather than papering over them with `Core`
repositories.trends_summary: Steady cadence of automated scraping and manual curation of platform release pages continues.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Release page curation** remains an active workflow: [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) continues coordinating with tool maintainers to produce accurate, human-readable summaries from GitHub release notes, as seen in the nine-tool batch in [#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641)
- **Documentation quality feedback** is emerging, with [#3664](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3664) being an example of the community pushing for stdlib-first examples — a recurring theme given the website's role as the primary learning resource for the language
repositories.contributors_summary: sabine authored platform release changelogs and the Backstage OCaml post; opale raised a documentation quality issue.
repositories.contributors:
- [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) authored the platform release changelogs PR ([#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641)) coordinating with tool maintainers across nine tools, and opened the Backstage OCaml wasm/wasi prototype data PR ([#3667](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3667))
- [opale](https://github.com/N1ark) opened issue [#3664](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3664) identifying a documentation concern around `Core` library usage in the GC docs and proposing a stdlib-only alternative

repositories.brief_summary: Active week with 26 merges including new packages, a security fix in albatross, and CI improvements for opam syntax validation.
repositories.new_features_summary: New packages include awso 0.9.0 (AWS API), slipshow LSP server, uring Res module, irky IRC library, and Solo5 network performance improvements.
repositories.new_features:
- **albatross 2.7.2** includes a security fix for unbounded resource usage in `albatross-console` when querying with a very high, negative, or early-date count while the ring buffer is full (OSEC-2026-09), contributed by [Reynir Björnsson](https://github.com/reynir) ([#29971](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29971))
- **Solo5 0.11.0** brings improved network performance on Linux via `eventfd(2)` for TAP interfaces (approximately 3x throughput increase in iperf3 benchmarks), `seccomp` filter support in `solo5-hvt`, and stricter C compiler warning flags across the codebase, released by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) ([#29965](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29965))
- **uring 2.14.0** adds a new `Uring.Res` module to handle result values without requiring `Obj.magic` for file descriptor operations (breaking change), plus new `send_zc` and `sendmsg_zc` zero-copy send operations, released by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#29954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29954))
- **slipshow 0.11.0** adds a built-in LSP server (`slipshow lsp`) supporting diagnostics, hover documentation, go-to-definition for IDs, occurrence highlighting, and completion; also adds a visual indicator for previewer connection state and allows frontmatter in included files, released by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) ([#29951](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29951))
- **irky 0.1** is a new IRC client library forked from `irc-client`, providing direct-style IO backends for both Unix and Eio with optional SSL/TLS support, released by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) ([#29925](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29925))
- **awso 0.9.0** (6 packages) is an OCaml AWS API library covering 400+ services auto-generated from botocore, with synchronous (`awso-sync`) and async (`awso-async`) backends, released by [Michael Bacarella](https://github.com/mbacarella) ([#29921](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29921))
- **opentelemetry 0.91.1** (9 packages) fixes lazy initialization of random generators and respects `~parent:P_none` in trace, released by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) ([#29953](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29953))
- **smtml 0.28.0** adds Z3 native API implementations for `Re.allchar`, `Re.diff`, and remaining `String.replace` functions, plus tail-recursive symbol traversal and `Bitv128` conversions, released by [@filipeom](https://github.com/filipeom) ([#29966](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29966))
- **transom 0.1.0** is a new package providing OCaml sidecar glue for Tauri-style desktop apps, connecting a webview frontend to an OCaml process over newline-delimited JSON, released by [@funwithcthulhu](https://github.com/funwithcthulhu) ([#29963](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29963))
- **contract 0.1.0** is a new package for typed HTTP API contracts in OCaml with OpenAPI 3.0.3 output, released by [@funwithcthulhu](https://github.com/funwithcthulhu) ([#29961](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29961))
- **doctor 0.3.0** is an updated release of the OCaml development environment diagnostic CLI tool, released by [@funwithcthulhu](https://github.com/funwithcthulhu) ([#29962](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29962))
- **openrouter_api 0.1.0** is a new Async-based OCaml client for the OpenRouter LLM aggregation API, supporting chat completions with streaming, tool calling, multimodal content, embeddings, and model listing, released by [mt_caret](https://github.com/mt-caret) ([#29914](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29914))
- **rtree 0.2.0** adds `remove` functions and a `bounds` function for querying overall tree dimensions, plus fixes for `values` returning incomplete results, released by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) via [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#29959](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29959))
- **Windows GHA CI** now correctly returns a red status on opam file syntax errors by running `opam lint` on each changed package, fixing a longstanding issue where the CI would appear green despite invalid opam files, implemented by [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid) ([#29964](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29964), fixes [#29513](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29513))
- **caqti-lwt 2.3.0** has its `lwt < 6~` upper bound relaxed via a metadata-only change, enabling packages like Dream to install `caqti-lwt` with Lwt 6 and OCaml 5.5, by [@funwithcthulhu](https://github.com/funwithcthulhu) ([#29937](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29937))
- **ctypes** opam files synced with upstream to produce relocatable `dune-package` files using modern dune defaults, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#29956](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29956))
- **async_ssl** v0.16.1-2 and v0.17.0-2 restore the `-Wno-implicit-function-declaration` C flag suppression that was accidentally dropped, fixing builds on CentOS 10 and other platforms with strict GCC defaults, by [Michael Bacarella](https://github.com/mbacarella) ([#29939](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29939))

Urkedal](https://github.com/paurkedal) clarifying the minimal scope of such changes and the policy of avoiding unnecessary releases to limit CI load

repositories.brief_summary: A highly active week delivering new Promise, Fetch, and Abort API bindings, major Dom breaking changes, compiler dispatch-loop fixes, and Popover API support.
repositories.new_features_summary: New Promise, Fetch, and Abort modules landed alongside Popover API support and a native Wasm stack-switching effects backend.
repositories.new_features:
- A new **Promise module** was added providing type-safe bindings to JavaScript promises, closing long-standing [#2031](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2031), with Lwt interop via `Js_of_ocaml_lwt.Promise` (`to_lwt` / `of_lwt`).
- The **Fetch API** is now fully bound in a new `Fetch` module, closing the oldest open feature request [#596](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/596) (from 2017), together with typed `Abort` (`AbortController`/`AbortSignal`) primitives for cancellation.
- **Promise-typed `Dom_html` bindings** were added for `requestFullscreen`, `requestPointerLock`, `exitFullscreen`, `mediaElement.play`, `imageElement.decode`, and `Animation.{finished,ready}`; `mediaElement.play` now returns `unit Promise.t` (breaking change).
- The **Popover API** was implemented in `Dom_html` via [#1734](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1734), covering `showPopover`, `hidePopover`, `togglePopover`, `popoverTargetElement`/`popoverTargetAction`, and toggle events.
- A new **`--effects=native` backend** for `wasm_of_ocaml` was introduced, using the WebAssembly Stack Switching proposal for best performance on Chrome 148+ and recent Node.js canary builds.
repositories.activity_summary: 56 commits spanning 99 files with 4204 insertions landed, touching the compiler, runtime, library bindings, CI, and benchmarks.
repositories.activity:
- **Breaking changes** were introduced in the `Dom` and `Dom_html` modules to align Promise-typed APIs with the new Promise module, with CI fixed to use branches adapted to these changes.
- The **compiler's closure generation** (`generate_closure.ml`) was heavily refactored: dispatch per effects mode was split, closure-emission was collapsed into a record, and CPS closures are now stored directly in `cps_pair`, closing [#2122](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2122).
- A **flat dispatch loop** was introduced in the compiler to bound statement-nesting depth, preventing JavaScript engine stack overflows on parsers with deep mutual recursion, resolving [#2122](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2122).
- A **UGEINT bytecode lowering bug** was fixed (returned wrong result on equal operands), accompanied by a regression test.
- **CI infrastructure** was improved with a more robust Docker compilation process and stricter memory measurement (`%M` instead of `%R`) in benchmarks.
- **Contributing documentation** was expanded with detailed library binding conventions for underscore usage in method names, preventing future name-mangling bugs.
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion centred on the 6.4 release milestone, WebGL2 support interest, and wasm_of_ocaml function export workflows.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- The **js_of_ocaml 6.4 release** tracking issue [#2241](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2241) was opened by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo), gathering the batch of new APIs landed this week under an upcoming release milestone.
- **WebGL2 support** ([#1226](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1226)) was revisited: [Christophe Raffalli](https://github.com/craff) confirmed continuing interest but no bandwidth for a PR at this time.
- A user question about **exporting OCaml functions from wasm_of_ocaml** ([#2028](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2028)) surfaced a documentation gap that remains open.
repositories.trends_summary: The week reflects a sustained effort to close the gap between js_of_ocaml and the modern JavaScript API surface, particularly async primitives.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Async API completeness** is the dominant theme: Promise, Fetch, and AbortController were all landed together as an interdependent tier, and the API-STATUS roadmap was updated to reflect these closures.
- **Compiler output quality** is an active concern: the flat dispatch loop work in [#2122](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2122) addresses real-world engine limits (SpiderMonkey "too much recursion") driven by Menhir-generated parsers.
- **Wasm effects backends** are expanding: the new `--effects=native` stack-switching mode joins JSPI and CPS, tracking the evolving WebAssembly standards landscape.
repositories.issues_summary: Four issues were closed this week, all tied to new library bindings or compiler bug fixes.
repositories.good_first_issues:
- [#2028](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2028) asks how to **export specific OCaml functions** from a `wasm_of_ocaml` build and is labelled `documentation`, making it a good entry point for someone familiar with the toolchain.
- [#1226](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1226) requests **WebGL2 bindings** and has a preliminary implementation from [Christophe Raffalli](https://github.com/craff) that could be picked up by a contributor with WebGL experience.
repositories.contributors_summary: Four contributors committed code this week, led by Hugo Heuzard and Jérôme Vouillon.
repositories.contributors:
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) drove the bulk of compiler work (37 commits) including the flat dispatch loop, CPS closure refactoring, and Popover API helpers for [#2122](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2122).
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) contributed 18 commits covering Dom/Dom_html breaking changes, dispatch loop refinements, CI hardening, and the `onbeforeunload` fix for [#1436](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1436).
- [Sylvain Boilard](https://github.com/SylvainBoilard) landed the initial Popover API implementation via [#1734](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1734).
- [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) is credited with the Promise feature request [#2031](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2031) that was resolved this week.

repositories.brief_summary: Runtime performance improvements, new caml_region C API, type system fixes for atomic/unboxed records, and Merlin sync landed this week.
repositories.new_features_summary: New caml_region C API, optional [@@flatten_floats], atomic record unboxing restrictions, and --enable-keyword-edition configure option.
repositories.new_features:
- New **`caml_region_begin` / `caml_region_end` C API** functions added by [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan), providing explicit region management hooks for the C runtime ([#6142](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6142))
- **`[@@flatten_floats]`** is now an optional annotation rather than automatic for mixed `float`/`float64` records by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa), giving users explicit control over the memory representation of heterogeneous float records ([#6106](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6106))
- Records with **`[@atomic]` fields** no longer receive unboxed versions by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa), preventing unsound interactions between atomicity and unboxed representations ([#6124](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6124))
- **`--enable-keyword-edition`** configure option added for the 5.4 branch by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js), allowing builds to opt-in to keyword edition support ([#6140](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6140))
- **`quick_stat`** runtime function optimized by [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) to reduce overhead in the GC statistics path ([#6139](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6139))
- **Profile info collection** is now skipped when profiling is disabled by [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan), reducing overhead in non-profiling builds ([#6141](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6141))
- Fixed **non-empty array types with bottom kind** being incorrectly created by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp), preventing ill-typed array constructions from passing the type checker ([#6134](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6134))
- A couple of **flags missing from `ocamlnat`/`expect.opt` drivers** were added by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) ([#6159](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6159))
- **Merlin synced to `5.2.0minus-39`** by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski), pulling in upstream Merlin changes including new `jkind` scannable constant, updated `cmi_format` to use `persistent_signature`, and `compilation_unit` name mangling overrides ([#6136](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6136))
repositories.activity_summary: Several PRs opened targeting sort variable correctness for records, plus ongoing large efforts in functorization, layout polymorphism, and CFG optimization.
repositories.activity:
- Newly opened **sort variable and any-kind record fixes** by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) include fixing sort variables for variable-representation constructor patterns ([#6156](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6156)), fixing any-record refined to all-`float64` getting wrong `Record_boxed` representation ([#6143](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6143)), and creating sort variables for record fields with kind `any` ([#6145](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6145))
- Draft PR **tracing variable availability through the compiler** opened by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon), a +1612/-40 change aimed at improving debugger support for OxCaml programs ([#6158](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6158))
- Draft **CFG merge functions (Identical Code Folding)** opened by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) ([#6148](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6148))

repositories.discussions_summary: A question about the recommended idiom for uniqueness-checked destruction with read accessors prompted a pointer to borrowing documentation.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [Pedro N. Rodriguez H](https://github.com/pedronahum) opened issue [#6146](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6146) asking about the recommended idiom for `t @ unique`-checked destruction with read accessors, touching on the interaction between uniqueness and borrowing in OxCaml's mode system; the discussion pointed to existing borrowing documentation as the current answer
repositories.trends_summary: Unboxed record representation correctness is receiving focused attention, and runtime performance micro-optimizations are landing steadily.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Unboxed record representation correctness** is a recurring theme, with multiple PRs this week tightening rules around which records receive unboxed versions — including `[@atomic]` fields ([#6124](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6124)), mixed float records ([#6106](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6106)), and `any`-kinded fields ([#6143](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6143), [#6145](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6145), [#6156](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6156))
- **Runtime performance micro-optimizations** continue to land in small focused commits, covering GC statistics ([#6139](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6139)) and profiling infrastructure ([#6141](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6141))
- **CFG-level compiler optimizations** are growing with both jump threading ([#6104](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6104)) and identical code folding ([#6148](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6148)) in flight simultaneously
- **Merlin synchronization** with the main OxCaml compiler continues to be an ongoing integration task, with this week's sync ([#6136](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6136)) bringing in substantial upstream changes
repositories.contributors_summary: Seven contributors merged work this week, with Ryan Tjoa, Stephen Dolan, and Jakub Bachurski each landing multiple changes.

- [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) merged the Merlin sync to `5.2.0minus-39` ([#6136](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6136)), a large integration bringing in updated Merlin APIs
