repositories.brief_summary: A patch compatibility fix for Dune's new OCaml patch engine was opened, and a Dune build issue with ocaml-variants was resolved.
repositories.activity_summary: One PR opened to fix patch line-number drift; one issue closed after upstream Dune fix landed; one issue tracking a package-version bump remains open.
repositories.activity:
- [Christian Persson](https://github.com/saser) opened [#51](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/51) to update `ignore-opam.patch` so that `oxcaml-compiler` builds correctly under Dune's new pure-OCaml patch implementation ([ocaml/dune#14177](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14177)); the existing patch had drifted out of sync with `Makefile.common-ox` by ~20 lines, which was previously masked by GNU patch's fuzzy matching but broke with the stricter OCaml implementation
- Issue [#48](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/48) (`ocaml-variants` fails to build in a Dune project with OxCaml) was resolved and closed on 2026-05-28 following a fix by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter), acknowledged by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js)
- Issue [#46](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/46) tracking the bump to `v0.18~preview.130.100+614` (which resolves [janestreet/ocaml_intrinsics_kernel#5](https://github.com/janestreet/ocaml_intrinsics_kernel/issues/5)) remains open; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) confirmed the release is prepared in the dev branch and is expected to land on main imminently
repositories.discussions_summary: Upstream Dune's shift to a strict OCaml patch engine drove both the closure of issue #48 and the opening of PR #51.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- On [#51](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/51) [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) raised the question of whether OxCaml maintainers plan to keep patch line numbers accurate going forward, or whether Dune should enable fuzzy patch matching — offering to make that change if it would ease maintenance
- On [#46](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/46) [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) clarified that the `v0.18~preview.130.100+614` packages are assembled via automated tooling and that ~30 packages flagged by [Artem Pianykh](https://github.com/artempyanykh) as missing are being tracked on the dev branch before promotion to main
repositories.trends_summary: Dune's migration to a pure-OCaml patch implementation is surfacing patch-drift issues in opam package definitions.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Dune's replacement of GNU patch with a pure-OCaml implementation ([ocaml/dune#14177](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14177)) is exposing previously hidden line-number drift in patches bundled with opam packages; both the closure of [#48](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/48) and the opening of [#51](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/51) trace back to this upstream change, suggesting repository maintainers may need to audit other patches for similar drift
- The automated tooling used to promote Jane Street preview releases to the opam repository (illustrated in [#46](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/46)) occasionally lags for a subset of packages, pointing to a recurring coordination challenge around large batch version bumps
repositories.contributors_summary: Christian Persson opened a patch-fix PR; Ali Caglayan fixed the ocaml-variants build issue; David Allsopp triaged and responded to both open issues.
repositories.contributors:
- [Christian Persson](https://github.com/saser) reported the `ocaml-variants` build breakage in [#48](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/48) and followed up by opening [#51](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/51) with a concrete patch fix after verifying it against a Dune nightly build
- [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) delivered the fix that closed [#48](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/48) and provided review context on [#51](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/51), explaining Dune's current patching behaviour and offering to adjust fuzzy-matching settings if needed
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) triaged both [#46](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/46) and [#48](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/48), providing timeline information on the `v0.18~preview.130.100+614` promotion and acknowledging the fix in [#48](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/48)
- [Artem Pianykh](https://github.com/artempyanykh) filed [#46](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/46) with a detailed list of ~30 packages missing from the `130.100+614` release, providing useful diagnostic groundwork for the automated tooling team

repositories.brief_summary: v2.14.0 released; AT_FDCWD handling moved to C eliminating Obj.magic; several bug fixes and new op PRs opened.
repositories.new_features_summary: AT_FDCWD now handled in C stubs; fd params changed to option type; taskrun_flag docs improved.
repositories.new_features:
- **`AT_FDCWD` handling moved to C stubs**, eliminating use of `Obj.magic` in the OCaml layer. Functions `openat2`, `linkat`, `unlink`, `mkdirat`, and `statx` now accept `Unix.file_descr option` where `None` maps to `AT_FDCWD` natively in C, via [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)'s [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/128). The `at_fdcwd` binding and discovery of `AT_FDCWD` via `discover.ml` are removed.
- Fixed **`dio_offset_align` statx getter** that was incorrectly returning `stx_dio_mem_align` instead of the correct `stx_dio_offset_align` field — a copy-paste error affecting direct I/O alignment queries ([#141](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/141)).
- Improved **`taskrun_flag` documentation** in `uring.mli`, now explaining its interaction with `coop_taskrun`, `defer_taskrun`, and `get_cqe_nonblocking`, including the `IORING_SQ_TASKRUN` kernel flag semantics ([4026e90](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/commit/4026e90aec66f2cba94f596813124dbb7853072f)).
- **v2.14.0 release** published by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), documenting breaking changes (`Uring.Res` module), new ops (`send_zc`, `sendmsg_zc`), and bug fixes from prior work ([#140](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/140)).
repositories.activity_summary: Several PRs opened for new io_uring ops and fixes; lintcstubs-arity CI dependency corrected; io_uring_submit error reporting improved.
repositories.activity:
- **New ops PR** opened by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) adding `shutdown`, `socket`, `renameat`, and `symlinkat` operations with individual commits and tests, fixing the symlink-follow test for current liburing ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147)) — not yet merged.
- **Statx `Attr` API bug** found and PR opened by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm): the supported check had an inverted condition, uncovered by new tests ([#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142)) — not yet merged.
- **`Region.init` bounds check** added to raise early on invalid parameters rather than failing on subsequent access, opened by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#145](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/145)) — opened this week, merged the following week.
- **`io_uring_submit` error reporting** fixed by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5): previously a negative return value was silently passed through; now raises a Unix error ([#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/144)) — opened this week, merged the following week.
- **Debug message fixes** in `ocaml_uring_unregister_buffers` correcting missing `%p` format args and wrong sign for errno, opened by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#146](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/146)) — opened this week, merged the following week.
- **`lintcstubs-arity` dependency** changed from depopt to hard test dependency so CI reliably catches missing primitive regenerations; primitives header regenerated ([#143](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/143)).
repositories.discussions_summary: PR #142 prompted a discussion about redesigning the Statx Attr API to use abstract types instead of int64.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- The **`Statx.Attr` API design** came under scrutiny in [#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142): [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) suggested the `check` function signature should use the abstract `t` type rather than raw `int64` arguments, and proposed aligning it with `Attr.mem`; he also asked [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) for input on the proposed change. [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) pushed a revised implementation making the type abstract and adding `Flags.(=)` and `of/to_int` conversions.
- In [#143](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/143), [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) raised the question of whether `lintcstubs-arity` needs to remain optional at all, suggesting it could be optional in the dune file but mandatory in opam to simplify testing on unreleased compilers.
- In [#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/144), [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) flagged that the `caml_unix_error` function is unavailable on OCaml 4.x and raised the question of dropping OCaml 4.x support for this library, given Eio only targets OCaml 5.x.
repositories.trends_summary: Ongoing effort to eliminate unsafe OCaml patterns (Obj.magic) and improve API type safety in the library.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- A sustained effort to **eliminate `Obj.magic`** from the codebase is visible across [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/128) (AT_FDCWD), and is referenced as a motivating factor in [#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142) (Attr API abstraction). The library is progressively moving unsafe C interop into C stubs rather than OCaml-side casts.
- The **OCaml 5.x-only question** emerged in [#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/144): since Eio (the primary consumer) requires OCaml 5.x, there is discussion about dropping OCaml 4.x compatibility, which would allow using `caml_unix_error` and other 5.x-only APIs directly.
- **New io_uring operation coverage** is expanding: `send_zc`, `sendmsg_zc` were part of the v2.14.0 release, and [#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147) adds `shutdown`, `socket`, `renameat`, and `symlinkat`.
repositories.contributors_summary: Anil Madhavapeddy drove most new PRs and bug fixes; Thomas Leonard contributed the release, error reporting fix, and review feedback.
repositories.contributors:
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) opened and merged the `AT_FDCWD` cleanup ([#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/128)), fixed the `dio_offset_align` getter bug ([#141](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/141)), fixed the lintcstubs-arity CI dependency ([#143](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/143)), and opened PRs for the Statx attribute inversion bug ([#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142)), new ops ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147)), bounds check ([#145](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/145)), and debug message fixes ([#146](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/146)).
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) prepared and published the v2.14.0 release ([#140](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/140)), opened the `io_uring_submit` error reporting fix ([#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/144)), and provided review comments on [#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142) and [#143](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/143) including suggestions on the `Attr` API design and `lintcstubs-arity` dependency approach.
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) authored the original `AT_FDCWD` elimination PR ([#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/128)) which was merged this week, removing the last `Obj.magic` usage in the library.
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) was consulted by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) for input on the proposed `Statx.Attr` API changes in [#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142).

repositories.brief_summary: Performance improvements to eio_linux via io_uring flags and a file descriptor security fix for eio_posix.
repositories.new_features_summary: io_uring DEFER_TASKRUN and SINGLE_ISSUER flags improve HTTP throughput; MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC fixes fd security on POSIX.
repositories.new_features:
- `eio_linux` now uses **`IORING_SETUP_DEFER_TASKRUN`** and **`IORING_SETUP_SINGLE_ISSUER`** when creating io_uring rings, improving HTTP benchmark throughput by roughly 100k req/s on supported hardware (from ~350k to ~450k); falls back gracefully to plain `Uring.create` on older kernels (pre-Linux 6.1) via an `EINVAL` catch ([#841](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/841), [#842](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/842))
- `eio_posix` now passes **`MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC`** to `recvmsg` when receiving file descriptors, ensuring received fds are close-on-exec atomically; falls back to calling `Unix.set_close_on_exec` manually on macOS where the flag is absent ([#843](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/843))
repositories.activity_summary: Two long-running PRs for chown support and incremental directory reading remain open, and a new issue tracks disabling fixed buffers by default.
repositories.activity:
- **Ongoing:** [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris)'s `chown` support PR ([#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781)) continues to receive review from [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), with discussion around making `uid` and `gid` optional and documentation of the `follow` parameter
- **Ongoing:** [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris)'s draft PR for **incremental directory reading** ([#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821)) received detailed review feedback from [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) covering API naming, error handling scope, batching of thread jobs, and resource cleanup
- **New issue:** [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) opened [#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845) to track disabling **fixed buffers by default** in `eio_linux`, after a ZFS kernel bug was reported that requires a reboot when fixed buffers are used; this followed the closure of the earlier io_uring concerns issue ([#797](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/797))
- Benchmark Dockerfiles were updated to use a recent version of `ocaml-uring` and to work around a change in `opam install --deps-only` behaviour ([#844](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/844))
- PR [#840](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/840) (an earlier attempt at uring performance flags) was closed without merging in favour of the cleaner split into [#841](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/841) and [#842](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/842)
repositories.discussions_summary: Cross-platform MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC availability and io_uring fixed buffers causing ZFS kernel crashes were the main topics.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- The `MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC` PR ([#843](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/843)) prompted a discussion between [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) about macOS compatibility: [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) initially noted the flag would not work on macOS, while [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) clarified it is in POSIX but listed as optional for implementations; the final solution uses compile-time detection and falls back to `set_close_on_exec` on macOS
- Issue [#797](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/797) (io_uring security and stability concerns) was closed after [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) triaged the specific ZFS fixed-buffer kernel crash into a dedicated tracking issue ([#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845))
- A comment on issue [#801](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/801) (Uring shared across fork) from [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) noted that the newly merged `IORING_SETUP_SINGLE_ISSUER` flag ([#841](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/841)) should now generate an `EEXIST` error in child processes that attempt to use the parent's ring
repositories.trends_summary: Sustained focus on io_uring performance tuning and on hardening file descriptor safety across POSIX and Linux backends.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- A pattern of **targeted io_uring flag adoption** is emerging: three consecutive PRs ([#840](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/840), [#841](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/841), [#842](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/842)) explored `IORING_SETUP_SINGLE_ISSUER`, `IORING_SETUP_COOP_TASKRUN`, and `IORING_SETUP_DEFER_TASKRUN`, with careful benchmarking and graceful fallback for older kernels
- **Cross-platform correctness** is receiving attention: the `MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC` fix ([#843](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/843)) and the `chown` PR ([#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781)) both required platform-specific handling between Linux and macOS
- The new issue [#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845) indicates a shift towards **safer defaults** for `eio_linux`, moving away from features (fixed buffers) that offer marginal performance gains but risk kernel bugs
repositories.contributors_summary: Thomas Leonard drove all merged work this week; Patrick Ferris had active PRs under review; Anil Madhavapeddy provided review input.
repositories.contributors:
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) authored and merged all four PRs this week: `IORING_SETUP_SINGLE_ISSUER` ([#841](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/841)), `IORING_SETUP_DEFER_TASKRUN` ([#842](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/842)), `MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC` for eio_posix ([#843](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/843)), and Docker benchmark updates ([#844](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/844)); also opened issue [#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845) and closed [#797](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/797) after triage
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) had two PRs active under review: the long-running `chown` support ([#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781)) and the draft incremental directory reading implementation ([#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821)); also tested the DEFER_TASKRUN benchmark on Linux 6.12.77 and reported results on [#842](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/842)
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) reviewed PR [#843](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/843), raising and then confirming the macOS portability question around `MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC`

repositories.brief_summary: Patrick Ferris opened a cmdliner migration PR and closed three stale PRs to tidy the backlog.
repositories.activity_summary: CLI argument parsing is being migrated to cmdliner, with stale PRs from 2023–2024 closed.
repositories.activity:
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) opened [#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75) to **migrate the `meio` executable to cmdliner** for argument parsing, taking advantage of a dependency already present in the project and bumping the ocamlformat version alongside it.
- Three **stale PRs were closed without merging**: the 2024 Eio tracing events port ([#71](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/71)), the 2023 OCaml 5.1.0 compatibility and `meio-runtime-events` generalisation ([#67](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/67)), and an earlier attempt at improved usage errors via `Stdlib.Arg` ([#74](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/74)) — all superseded by or conflicting with the current cmdliner approach.
repositories.trends_summary: Consolidation around cmdliner for CLI parsing, replacing earlier Stdlib.Arg and ad-hoc approaches.
repositories.emerging_trends: - The closure of [#74](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/74) (Stdlib.Arg) in favour of [#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75) (cmdliner) points to a deliberate move toward a **richer CLI parsing library** that was already a declared dependency, cleaning up argument handling for the `meio` executable.
repositories.contributors_summary: Patrick Ferris was the sole contributor, opening one new PR and closing three stale ones.
repositories.contributors: - [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) opened the cmdliner migration PR ([#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75)), and closed three long-standing stale PRs ([#74](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/74), [#71](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/71), [#67](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/67)) to reduce backlog clutter.

group_summaries.brief_summary: opam-repository merged 26 PRs with new packages and a security fix; ocaml.org published changelogs for nine platform tools.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: New packages include awso 0.9.0, irky IRC library, uring Res module, slipshow LSP server, and solo5 network improvements; nine platform changelogs published on ocaml.org.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **albatross 2.7.2** includes a security fix (OSEC-2026-09) for unbounded resource usage in `albatross-console` when querying with extreme count values while the ring buffer is full, contributed by [Reynir Björnsson](https://github.com/reynir) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29971](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29971))
- **solo5 0.11.0** brings ~3x network throughput improvement on Linux via `eventfd(2)` for TAP interfaces, `seccomp` filter support in `solo5-hvt`, and stricter C compiler warning flags, released by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29965](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29965))
- **uring 2.14.0** adds a new `Uring.Res` module for result values without `Obj.magic`, plus `send_zc` and `sendmsg_zc` zero-copy send operations (breaking change), released by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29954))
- **slipshow 0.11.0** adds a built-in LSP server (`slipshow lsp`) with diagnostics, hover, go-to-definition, occurrence highlighting, and completion support, released by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29951](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29951))
- **irky 0.1** is a new IRC client library forked from `irc-client`, providing direct-style IO for both Unix and Eio with optional SSL/TLS, released by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29925](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29925))
- **awso 0.9.0** (6 packages) provides OCaml bindings for 400+ AWS API services auto-generated from botocore, with synchronous and async backends, released by [Michael Bacarella](https://github.com/mbacarella) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29921](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29921))
- **transom 0.1.0** is a new OCaml sidecar library for Tauri-style desktop apps, connecting a webview frontend to an OCaml process over newline-delimited JSON, released by [@funwithcthulhu](https://github.com/funwithcthulhu) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29963](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29963))
- **contract 0.1.0** is a new typed HTTP API contract library with OpenAPI 3.0.3 output, released by [@funwithcthulhu](https://github.com/funwithcthulhu) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29961](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29961))
- **openrouter_api 0.1.0** is a new Async-based client for the OpenRouter LLM aggregation API with streaming, tool calling, and multimodal content support, released by [mt_caret](https://github.com/mt-caret) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29914](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29914))
- **smtml 0.28.0** adds Z3 native API implementations for `Re.allchar`, `Re.diff`, and `String.replace`, plus tail-recursive symbol traversal and `Bitv128` conversions, released by [@filipeom](https://github.com/filipeom) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29966](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29966))
- **opentelemetry 0.91.1** (9 packages) fixes lazy initialization of random generators and respects `~parent:P_none` in traces, released by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29953](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29953))
- **Windows GHA CI** now returns a red status on opam file syntax errors by running `opam lint` on changed packages, fixing a longstanding gap ([ocaml/opam-repository#29513](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29513)), implemented by [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29964](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29964))
- **caqti-lwt 2.3.0** has its `lwt < 6~` upper bound relaxed, enabling packages like Dream to install `caqti-lwt` with Lwt 6 and OCaml 5.5, by [@funwithcthulhu](https://github.com/funwithcthulhu) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29937](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29937))
- **async_ssl** v0.16.1-2 and v0.17.0-2 restore `-Wno-implicit-function-declaration` suppression, fixing builds on CentOS 10 and GCC 14+ platforms, by [Michael Bacarella](https://github.com/mbacarella) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29939](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29939))
- **Platform release changelogs** for nine tools published on ocaml.org: dune-release 2.2.1, mdx 2.5.2, merlin v5.7-505~preview, merlin v5.7.1-504, ocaml-lsp 1.26.0-5.5~preview, OCaml 5.5.0~beta1, ocamlformat 0.29.0, odoc 3.2.0, opam-publish 3.0.0, and utop 2.17.0, by [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641))
- **Dune 3.24.0~alpha0 release page** automatically scraped to ocaml.org, documenting promotion fixes, directory targets promoted to stable, and the new `%{pkg:<package>:<section>:<path>}` pform ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3666](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3666))
- **OCaml Planet** updated with Anil's `.plan` post covering Pint of Science, OxCaml dissertations, and TESSERA 1.1, plus OCaml Weekly News for 26 May 2026 ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3663](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3663), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3665](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3665))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **ocaml/opam-repository** saw 26 merges this week covering new package releases, a security fix, dependency constraint updates, and a CI reliability improvement for opam syntax validation
- **ocaml/ocaml.org** published platform release changelogs for nine tools, added a Dune 3.24.0~alpha0 release page via automated scraping, and received a documentation quality issue about `Core` library usage in GC examples
- Combined contributor activity across both repositories included approximately 20 individuals spanning package authors, reviewers, and infrastructure maintainers
- The week's opam-repository activity spans multiple domains: systems programming (solo5, uring, albatross), web frameworks (caqti-lwt, awso), formal methods (z3, smtml, symex), and developer tooling (slipshow LSP, doctor)
- OCaml 5.5 compatibility surfaced as a cross-cutting concern, with several opam-repository packages updating constraints and ocaml.org publishing the OCaml 5.5.0~beta1 changelog
group_summaries.activity_summary: New packages pending merge, a dune 3.24.0~alpha0 pre-release draft, and ocaml.org documentation quality feedback characterized ongoing work.
group_summaries.activity:
- **dune 3.24.0~alpha0 pre-release** opened as a draft by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) covering 18 packages with new `%{pkg:<package>:<section>:<path>}` pform, directory targets promoted to stable, relocatable compiler enabled by default, and removal of deprecated `(lang coq)` in favour of Rocq ([ocaml/opam-repository#29969](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29969))
- **object 0.1** (unified ELF/MACH-O/PE/COFF object file reader) opened by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) and blocked on CI failures including `Array.find_opt` availability on OCaml 4.11, dune lower bound issues, and ppc64/arm64 failures ([ocaml/opam-repository#29973](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29973))
- **awso 0.9.1** (7 packages with Eio backend) opened by [Michael Bacarella](https://github.com/mbacarella), requiring OCaml 5.3+ with tls >= 2.1.0 and mirage-crypto-rng >= 1.2.0 for CVE-2026-45388 mitigation ([ocaml/opam-repository#29967](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29967))
- **Dependency constraint fixes**: `jext 0.1.0` revdep syntax fix for `ez_api` by [Maxime Levillain](https://github.com/maxtori) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29957](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29957)); `albatross` syntax fix by [Reynir Björnsson](https://github.com/reynir) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29958](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29958))
- **Ecosystem compatibility releases**: `camlp5-buildscripts 0.07` adds bos 0.3.0 and OCaml 5.5 compatibility by [@chetmurthy](https://github.com/chetmurthy) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29950](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29950)); `lua-ml 0.9.5` by [Daniil Baturin](https://github.com/dmbaturin) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29945)); `ez_api 3.0.0` by [Maxime Levillain](https://github.com/maxtori) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29893](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29893))
- **coccinelle 1.3.1** moved `pcre` from `depends` to `depopts`, allowing installation on Debian systems as `libpcre3-dev` is removed, by [Denis Efremov](https://github.com/evdenis) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29974](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29974))
- **Formal methods tooling**: `z3 4.16.0` by [@filipeom](https://github.com/filipeom) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29933](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29933)); `symex 0.3` by [@redianthus](https://github.com/redianthus) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29972](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29972))
- **ctypes** opam files synced with upstream to produce relocatable `dune-package` files using modern dune defaults, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29956](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29956))
- **rtree 0.2.0** adds `remove` and `bounds` functions and fixes incomplete `values` results, submitted by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) and re-submitted by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) after an accidental browser closure ([ocaml/opam-repository#29959](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29959))
- **ocaml.org backstage post** on the wasm-of-ocaml WASI prototype opened by [sabine](https://github.com/sabine), awaiting review ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3667](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3667))
- **GC documentation feedback** opened by [opale](https://github.com/N1ark) arguing that stdlib-only patterns should be demonstrated rather than relying on `Core` library functions ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3664](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3664))
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Dune 3.24.0~alpha0** appears in both repositories: [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) opened the pre-release draft in opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#29969](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29969)) while the release page was simultaneously scraped to ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3666](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3666)), documenting overlapping content about directory targets and the new `%{pkg:...}` pform
- **OCaml 5.5.0~beta1** drives activity in both repositories: opam-repository packages (caqti-lwt, camlp5-buildscripts) are updating constraints for OCaml 5.5 / Lwt 6 compatibility ([ocaml/opam-repository#29937](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29937), [ocaml/opam-repository#29950](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29950)), while the beta1 changelog was published on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641))
- **Platform tooling changelogs** on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641)) cover tools whose packages appear in opam-repository, including odoc 3.2.0, ocamlformat 0.29.0, and opam-publish 3.0.0, reflecting the tight coupling between ocaml.org's release documentation and opam-repository package entries
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- The **jext.0.1.0 syntax error incident** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29957](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29957)) was reported by [Reynir Björnsson](https://github.com/reynir): a malformed opam file caused opam-mirror (a MirageOS unikernel) to crash with a fatal `Stdlib.Parsing.Parse_error`; this directly motivated [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid)'s CI fix in [ocaml/opam-repository#29964](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29964)
- **async_ssl and OpenSSL ENGINE API** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29939](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29939)): [Michael Bacarella](https://github.com/mbacarella) noted the gradual disappearance of `openssl/engine.h` from modern Linux distributions and nudged Jane Street to drop deprecated ENGINE API bindings in [janestreet/async_ssl#43](https://github.com/janestreet/async_ssl/pull/43)
- **coccinelle stdcompat upper bound** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29974](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29974)) surfaced compatibility blocks on OCaml 5.4 and 4.14.3 tracked upstream at [ocamllibs/stdcompat#70](https://github.com/ocamllibs/stdcompat/pull/70) and [ocamllibs/stdcompat#73](https://github.com/ocamllibs/stdcompat/pull/73), alongside the removal of `libpcre3-dev` from Debian
- **caqti-lwt metadata-only change** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29937](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29937)) prompted clarification from upstream maintainer [Petter A. Urkedal](https://github.com/paurkedal) on the policy of minimal-scope metadata changes to avoid unnecessary CI load
- **GC documentation and Core library use** ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3664](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3664)): [opale](https://github.com/N1ark) raised that the garbage collector documentation uses `Core` library functions absent from stdlib (e.g. `Gc.tune`), arguing the docs should highlight stdlib ergonomic gaps rather than deferring to `Core`
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Eio and OCaml 5+ backend adoption** continues: `irky 0.1` provides a direct-style Eio IO backend ([ocaml/opam-repository#29925](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29925)), `awso 0.9.1` adds an Eio AWS backend ([ocaml/opam-repository#29967](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29967)), and `uring 2.14.0` continues to underpin the Eio ecosystem ([ocaml/opam-repository#29954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29954))
- **OCaml 5.5 / Lwt 6 compatibility** is a recurring theme: `caqti-lwt` constraint relaxation ([ocaml/opam-repository#29937](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29937)), `camlp5-buildscripts` OCaml 5.5 support ([ocaml/opam-repository#29950](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29950)), and the OCaml 5.5.0~beta1 changelog on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641)) all reflect coordinated ecosystem-wide preparation
- **CI infrastructure reliability** receives incremental attention: the Windows GHA opam-lint fix ([ocaml/opam-repository#29964](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29964)) resolves a known gap demonstrated by the jext incident ([ocaml/opam-repository#29957](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29957))
- **Formal methods tooling** cluster: `z3 4.16.0`, `smtml 0.28.0`, and `symex 0.3` all landed in the same week ([ocaml/opam-repository#29933](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29933), [ocaml/opam-repository#29966](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29966), [ocaml/opam-repository#29972](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29972)), suggesting coordinated activity in the OCaml formal verification space
- **C compiler compatibility** continues to require patching: `async_ssl` needed flags restored for GCC 14+ ([ocaml/opam-repository#29939](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29939)) and `coccinelle` adjusted for `libpcre3-dev` removal from Debian ([ocaml/opam-repository#29974](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29974))
- **Documentation quality feedback** is emerging on ocaml.org, with [ocaml/ocaml.org#3664](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3664) representing community push for stdlib-first examples — a concern given the website's role as the primary OCaml learning resource

group_summaries.brief_summary: Active week across dune, js_of_ocaml, opam, merlin, and odoc with new BSD support, Promise/Fetch APIs, and HTTP repo test infrastructure.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: DragonFly BSD support in dune, Promise/Fetch/Abort APIs and Popover support in js_of_ocaml, HTTP repo testing in opam, and merlin signature-help fix.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **[ocaml/dune]** DragonFly BSD added as a recognised platform, fixing file-watcher and OS detection paths — [ocaml/dune#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14774) by [Michael Neumann](https://github.com/mneumann)
- **[ocaml/dune]** `dune pkg` solver now strips upper-bound version constraints on `dune` itself from opam `depends` formulas, preventing solver failures from stale `< 3.17` style bounds — [ocaml/dune#14638](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14638) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter), closes [ocaml/dune#14637](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14637)
- **[ocaml/dune]** `melange.ppx_runtime_libraries` field added to library stanzas for ppx rewriters needing separate native and Melange runtime libraries — [ocaml/dune#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14796) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro)
- **[ocaml/dune]** Shell command completion support (bash, zsh, PowerShell) opened via [ocaml/dune#14779](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14779) by [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS), using vendored cmdliner 2.1.1
- **[ocaml/merlin]** Signature-help with type aliases now correctly expands aliases to display function signatures — [ocaml/merlin#2067](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2067) by [Carine Morel](https://github.com/lyrm), fixes [ocaml/merlin#1927](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1927)
- **[ocaml/opam]** New `http-server` reftest command and `repository-http.test` suite enabling HTTP-based repository lifecycle testing — [ocaml/opam#6939](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6939) merged by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou)
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml]** New `Promise` module with type-safe JavaScript promise bindings and Lwt interop via `Js_of_ocaml_lwt.Promise`, closing [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2031](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2031)
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml]** New `Fetch` module binding the Fetch API with typed `Abort` primitives (`AbortController`/`AbortSignal`), closing the 2017 feature request [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#596](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/596)
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml]** Popover API implemented in `Dom_html` via [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1734](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1734) by [Sylvain Boilard](https://github.com/SylvainBoilard), covering `showPopover`, `hidePopover`, `togglePopover`, and toggle events
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml]** New `--effects=native` backend for `wasm_of_ocaml` using the WebAssembly Stack Switching proposal, targeting Chrome 148+ and recent Node.js canary builds
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Six of ten tracked repositories saw activity this week; [ocaml/ocamlfind](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind), [ocaml/opam-file-format](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-file-format), and [ocaml-dune/spawn](https://github.com/ocaml-dune/spawn) had no recorded commits or issues
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) was the highest-volume repository with 56 commits across 99 files (4204 insertions), delivering major new async API bindings and compiler improvements
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) continued broad-front development covering platform support, anonymous action expansion, Melange, watch-mode, and RPC
- [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) progressed the HTTP repository performance initiative with merged test infrastructure and queued PRs for tar library integration
- [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) merged a signature-help fix and closed a batch of long-standing issues (some from 2013–2021) after confirming fixes are in place
- [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) saw OxCaml feature PRs advance under review; layout support ([ocaml/odoc#1415](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1415)) was confirmed closed
- [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) received one new feature PR adding hover information for OCaml compiler warnings ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1606](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1606))
group_summaries.activity_summary: Dune expanded anonymous actions and Melange single-context support; opam queued tar library and conditional upgrade PRs; merlin closed stale issues with test coverage.
group_summaries.activity:
- **[ocaml/dune]** [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) expanded anonymous actions to cover pkg-config ([ocaml/dune#14782](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14782)), mdx dependency queries ([ocaml/dune#14787](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14787)), inline-test partitions ([ocaml/dune#14788](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14788)), and odoc dependency queries ([ocaml/dune#14786](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14786))
- **[ocaml/dune]** [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) advanced the `single-context-universal` Melange stack with installation rules ([ocaml/dune#14800](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14800)), conditional library tests ([ocaml/dune#14812](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14812)), and a cross-compilation target-env fix ([ocaml/dune#14817](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14817))
- **[ocaml/dune]** Scheduler file-watcher refactored in [ocaml/dune#14778](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14778): watcher events now flow through a thread-safe fiber channel; `FSEvents` startup and failure propagation fixed in [ocaml/dune#14764](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14764)
- **[ocaml/dune]** Cache copy-mode entries now have ctime refreshed on restore to retain recently-used entries ([ocaml/dune#14777](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14777)); build path separator inconsistency on Windows fixed ([ocaml/dune#14616](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14616))
- **[ocaml/dune]** [Danny Willems](https://github.com/dannywillems) opened [ocaml/dune#14798](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14798) fixing Emacs byte-compile warnings in editor integration and adding an Emacs CI workflow
- **[ocaml/opam]** [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) opened [ocaml/opam#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945) (tar library integration via new `OpamTar` module, READY TO MERGE) and [ocaml/opam#6949](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6949) (conditional hard upgrade for opam root, READY TO MERGE), both prerequisites for [ocaml/opam#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)
- **[ocaml/opam]** Lock file undefined variable fix opened as [ocaml/opam#6947](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6947) (fixing [ocaml/opam#6946](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6946)), approved by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb)
- **[ocaml/merlin]** Locate fix for let-punnings ([ocaml/merlin#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066)) by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) remains under review; introduces a tie-breaking mechanism in `Mbrowse` to disambiguate overlapping AST node locations, fixing [ocaml/merlin#1796](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1796)
- **[ocaml/merlin]** Emacs 31 compatibility update opened ([ocaml/merlin#2076](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2076)) by [Danny Willems](https://github.com/dannywillems) replacing obsolete `when-let` macro with `let` + `when`
- **[ocaml/merlin]** Batch of long-standing stale issues closed after verifying fixes are in place, including [ocaml/merlin#369](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/369), [ocaml/merlin#298](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/298), [ocaml/merlin#598](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/598), [ocaml/merlin#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1404), and others dating back to 2013
- **[ocaml/odoc]** OxCaml modalities PR [ocaml/odoc#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420) and zero-alloc annotation PR [ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422) remain under review; layout tracking issue [ocaml/odoc#1415](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1415) was closed
- **[ocaml/odoc]** Inline raise tag rendering fix opened as [ocaml/odoc#1440](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1440); multiline link fix opened as [ocaml/odoc#1439](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1439), both by [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d)
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml]** Compiler closure generation heavily refactored: dispatch per effects mode split, CPS closures stored directly in `cps_pair`, and a flat dispatch loop introduced to bound statement-nesting depth, resolving [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2122](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2122)
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml]** UGEINT bytecode lowering bug fixed (wrong result on equal operands) with regression test added
- **[ocaml/ocaml-lsp]** [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) opened [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1606](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1606) implementing hover information for OCaml compiler warnings, addressing feature request [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#717](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/717)
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **dune ↔ opam**: `dune pkg` solver now strips upper-bound version constraints on `dune` itself ([ocaml/dune#14638](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14638)), directly addressing package solver failures caused by stale opam `depends` bounds that restrict opam packages from using newer dune versions
- **dune ↔ odoc**: [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)'s anonymous action expansion includes odoc dependency queries ([ocaml/dune#14786](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14786)), indicating continued coordination between the dune build system and odoc's integration layer
- **merlin ↔ ocaml-lsp**: [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) self-closed [ocaml/merlin#2074](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2074) (get description of warnings in merlin) after determining the functionality is better served in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp), then opened [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1606](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1606) implementing hover on warnings — illustrating active boundary management between the two tools
- **merlin ↔ dune (Emacs tooling)**: [Danny Willems](https://github.com/dannywillems) opened Emacs compatibility fixes in both [ocaml/merlin#2076](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2076) (Emacs 31 `when-let` compatibility) and [ocaml/dune#14798](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14798) (Emacs byte-compile warnings + CI), coordinating Emacs tooling improvements across repositories
- **odoc ↔ merlin**: [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) reviewed PRs in both [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) ([ocaml/odoc#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420), [ocaml/odoc#1439](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1439), [ocaml/odoc#1440](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1440)) and [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) ([ocaml/merlin#2075](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2075)) this week
- **js_of_ocaml ↔ dune (shared issue reporter)**: [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) reported an intermittent missing `.d` dependency file issue in dune ([ocaml/dune#14767](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14767)) while also driving the bulk of js_of_ocaml development, reflecting cross-project contributor involvement
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **[ocaml/dune]** The 3.24.0 release tracker [ocaml/dune#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14620) remains open; `enabled_if` with `%{read:...}` in `(executable ...)` incorrectly rejected at dune language 3.23 was filed as [ocaml/dune#14789](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14789); a cache poisoning issue producing null-byte module names after the ocamldep-to-anonymous-actions migration was reported in [ocaml/dune#14767](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14767)
- **[ocaml/dune]** Sandbox-breaking of `git rev-parse` rules ([ocaml/dune#14723](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14723)) prompted discussion about exposing a `%{git-sha}` rule variable, leading [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) to open [ocaml/dune#14810](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14810)
- **[ocaml/merlin]** On [ocaml/merlin#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066), [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) identified a regression where the locate fix broke `type-enclosing` on punned values; [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) revised the approach to use a general tie-breaking function selecting pattern vs expression nodes based on query type
- **[ocaml/opam]** Review of [ocaml/opam#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945) by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) raised API design questions on type naming, I/O strategy, and the fact that directory metadata and empty directories are silently dropped when modifying archives; review also uncovered a subtle path prefix correctness bug in `OpamFilename.starts_with` ([ocaml/opam#6948](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6948))
- **[ocaml/odoc]** [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) recommended on [ocaml/odoc#1440](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1440) that the raise tag inlining fix should be implemented in the `Document` layer rather than the HTML backend, to avoid backend-specific workarounds

group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Anonymous actions replacing intermediate files in dune**: adoption is broadening across the build system (pkg-config, mdx, odoc, inline-test partitions, C compiler detection) via [ocaml/dune#14782](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14782), [ocaml/dune#14787](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14787), [ocaml/dune#14788](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14788), and [ocaml/dune#14786](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14786)
- **OxCaml feature parity in odoc**: multiple concurrent PRs ([ocaml/odoc#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420), [ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)) target modalities and zero-alloc annotations, building on the merged layout support ([ocaml/odoc#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410)); new external contributors [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d) and [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) joined this effort
- **Replacing external tool dependencies with native OCaml libraries in opam**: the `tar` tool replacement ([ocaml/opam#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945)) follows a broader pattern in [ocaml/opam#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625) of enabling in-process archive manipulation to improve performance and portability
- **Async API completeness in js_of_ocaml**: Promise, Fetch, and AbortController landed together as an interdependent tier, closing [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#596](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/596) (from 2017); the API-STATUS roadmap was updated to reflect these closures
- **Wasm effects backend expansion**: the new `--effects=native` stack-switching mode joins JSPI and CPS in `wasm_of_ocaml`, tracking the evolving WebAssembly standards landscape
- **Issue backlog triage in merlin**: long-standing issues (some from 2013–2021) are being closed by verifying existing fixes and adding regression tests, as seen in [ocaml/merlin#2073](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2073), [ocaml/merlin#2075](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2075), and the batch closure of [ocaml/merlin#298](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/298), [ocaml/merlin#369](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/369), [ocaml/merlin#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1404)
- **LSP hover richness**: [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1606](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1606) continues a pattern of enriching hover responses with OCaml-specific semantic information, making compiler diagnostic information accessible directly in editors

group_summaries.brief_summary: oxcaml/oxcaml landed runtime performance improvements, a new C API, type system fixes, and a Merlin sync; opam-repository addressed Dune patch compatibility.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: New caml_region C API, optional [@@flatten_floats], atomic record unboxing restrictions, and --enable-keyword-edition configure option.
group_summaries.new_features:
- New **`caml_region_begin` / `caml_region_end` C API** added by [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan), providing explicit region management hooks for the C runtime ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6142](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6142))
- **`[@@flatten_floats]`** is now an optional annotation rather than automatic for mixed `float`/`float64` records by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa), giving explicit control over memory representation of heterogeneous float records ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6106](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6106))
- Records with **`[@atomic]` fields** no longer receive unboxed versions by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa), preventing unsound interactions between atomicity and unboxed representations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6124](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6124))
- **`--enable-keyword-edition`** configure option added for the 5.4 branch by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js), allowing builds to opt-in to keyword edition support ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6140](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6140))
- **`quick_stat`** runtime function optimized by [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) to reduce GC statistics overhead ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6139](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6139))
- **Profile info collection** is now skipped when profiling is disabled by [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan), reducing overhead in non-profiling builds ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6141](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6141))
- Fixed **non-empty array types with bottom kind** being incorrectly created by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6134](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6134))
- **Merlin synced to `5.2.0minus-39`** by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski), pulling in updated `jkind` scannable constant, `cmi_format` using `persistent_signature`, and `compilation_unit` name mangling overrides ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6136](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6136))
- **Missing flags in `ocamlnat`/`expect.opt` drivers** added by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6159](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6159))
group_summaries.group_overview:

- [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin), [oxcaml/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/js_of_ocaml), and [janestreet/opam-repository](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository) had no recorded activity this week

Rodriguez H](https://github.com/pedronahum) opened [oxcaml/oxcaml#6146](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6146) asking about the recommended idiom for `t @ unique`-checked destruction with read accessors, touching on the interaction between uniqueness and borrowing in OxCaml's mode system; the discussion pointed to existing borrowing documentation

group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Unboxed record representation correctness** is receiving focused attention, with multiple PRs tightening rules around which records receive unboxed versions: `[@atomic]` fields ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6124](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6124)), mixed float records ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6106](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6106)), and `any`-kinded fields ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6143](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6143), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6145](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6145), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6156](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6156))

- **CFG-level compiler optimizations** are growing with both jump threading ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6104](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6104)) and identical code folding ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6148](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6148)) in flight simultaneously
