group_summaries.brief_summary: Ephemeron GC fixes landed, compiler profiling overhead eliminated, Windows Unix.select bug fixed, Antonin Décimo added as maintainer.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Compiler profiling overhead eliminated (up to 30% faster), Windows Unix.select fd-drop bug fixed, Char.escaped docs corrected.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Compiler profiling overhead eliminated** when `-dprofile`/`-dcounters`/`-dtimings` are not in use: the backend previously called `Profile.record_call` once per function per compilation phase regardless of whether profiling was enabled, causing up to 30% overhead in microbenchmarks with many small functions. The fix skips all profiling work unless requested; `profile_column` was moved from `Profile` to `Clflags`. ([ocaml/ocaml#14832](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14832))
- **Windows `Unix.select` file-descriptor drop fixed**: `socket_poll_add` returned the dispatched node rather than the (potentially updated) list head, silently dropping some fds from results and causing a memory leak. A regression test was added. ([ocaml/ocaml#14764](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14764))
- **`Char.escaped` documentation corrected** in `stdlib/char.mli`: the previous text incorrectly stated that double-quote characters are escaped; `Char.escaped '"'` returns `"\""` and double-quotes are not escaped. ([ocaml/ocaml#14836](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14836))
- **Two ephemeron GC correctness fixes merged**: [ocaml/ocaml#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734) fixes ephemeron orphaning during `Phase_sweep_ephe` (dangling pointers across GC cycles); [ocaml/ocaml#14733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14733) corrects per-domain ephemeron work counters and removes a pessimistic `ephe_next_round` call that caused unnecessary repeated work in multicore benchmarks.
- **Regression test for warning 60 location bug**: warning 60 (unused module) was previously reported at the wrong file location when a non-aliasable module substitution was involved. ([commit 1d2d55d](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/commit/1d2d55d2f0c30d12e907c0d200e3a4f29f643f5f), [ocaml/ocaml#12745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12745))
- **Manual option documentation improved**: `-match-context-rows` now shows its `number` argument in the options listing, with formatting and wording fixes in `unified-options.etex` and the `ocamlc`/`ocamlopt` man pages. ([ocaml/ocaml#14838](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14838))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- All activity this week was in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml); [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) had no activity.
- Six PRs were merged in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) spanning GC correctness, compiler performance, a Windows stdlib bug fix, CI improvements, cross-compilation fixes, and documentation.
- [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) was officially added as a maintainer of [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) via [ocaml/ocaml#14837](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14837).
- Thirteen contributors were active across code, review, benchmarking, and documentation roles.
- The modular IO RFC implementation ([ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775)) progressed as a draft PR, linking RFC work to upstream compiler development.
group_summaries.activity_summary: New maintainer added, CI improved, cross-compilation CI breakage fixed, and multiple GC/language/type-system proposals advancing in review.
group_summaries.activity:
- **New maintainer**: [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) added to `CONTRIBUTING.md` following a maintainer meeting decision ([ocaml/ocaml#14837](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14837)).
- **Newly opened — runtime events hang fix**: [Michael Bacarella](https://github.com/mbacarella) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14834](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14834) to fix a hang when an event exceeds the ring buffer size (originally reported in [ocaml/ocaml#13335](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13335)), proposing a fatal error instead of an infinite loop; under review by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche).
- **CI improvements**: [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) merged [ocaml/ocaml#14821](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14821) to disable "cold" tests in GitHub Actions and AppVeyor CI (permitted by [ocaml/ocaml#14713](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14713)); [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) hardened the C++ test to skip correctly on musl libc targets ([ocaml/ocaml#14827](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14827)).
- **Cross-compilation build fix**: [@shym](https://github.com/shym) merged [ocaml/ocaml#14835](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14835) fixing a broken `.depend` file caused by `ocamldep` misidentifying `Str` as `otherlibs/str` instead of `Ast_helper.Str` in `Typing_recovery_state` (introduced in [ocaml/ocaml#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241)), which had broken cross-compilation CI.
- **Build cleanup**: The fake `ocamlyacc.opt` file produced during cross-compilation builds was removed ([ocaml/ocaml#14833](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14833)).
- **Changelog/documentation housekeeping**: [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) added a missing PR number for `Fun.todo` ([ocaml/ocaml#14829](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14829)); [Enea Zani](https://github.com/Frenxys) fixed minor grammar across three documentation files ([ocaml/ocaml#14830](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14830)).
- **Ongoing — GC pacing**: [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) continues [ocaml/ocaml#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796), with [Eduardo Souza](https://github.com/udesou) providing macro-benchmark results (5×5 parameter sweep across 22 benchmarks) showing wall-time improvements across all tested configurations.
- **Ongoing — further ephemeron correctness**: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) has three additional ephemeron PRs in review: [ocaml/ocaml#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813) (no orphaned ephemerons across GC cycles), [ocaml/ocaml#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774) (wait for all domains to finish marking before a new ephemeron round), and [ocaml/ocaml#14745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14745) (test suite for known ephemeron bugs).
- **Ongoing — faster minor GC**: [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) updated [ocaml/ocaml#14571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14571) after addressing review comments; inlining the hot path in `oldify_one` offers 2–3% total runtime improvements in promotion-heavy workloads.
- **Ongoing — constructor eta-expansion**: [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14803](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14803) proposing auto eta-expansion of constructors in function-type contexts (e.g. `List.map Some [1;2;3]`).
- **Ongoing — extensible IO channels**: [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) has a draft [ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775) implementing extensible IO channels per the modular IO RFC.
- **Ongoing — type system alias following**: [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) continues [ocaml/ocaml#14746](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14746) to allow alias following when checking signature inclusion; [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) identified remaining issues in review.
- **Ongoing — C API bigarray exposure**: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) works on [ocaml/ocaml#14804](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14804) to expose `caml_ba_compare` and related bigarray functions outside `CAML_INTERNALS`.
- **Ongoing — libunix fixes**: [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) has [ocaml/ocaml#14740](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14740) with Unix library correctness fixes including missing blocking sections in `realpath` and `lockf`.
- **Ongoing — OCAMLRUNPARAM tests**: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14822](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14822) adding tests for current OCAMLRUNPARAM parsing behaviour as groundwork for follow-up cleanups.
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- The draft PR [ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775) by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) implements extensible IO channels as specified by the modular IO RFC tracked in [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs), representing the primary active link between the two repositories this week.
- [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) had no direct commits or PR activity during this period; RFC-driven work is advancing via implementation PRs in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml).
group_summaries.notable_discussions: - [ocaml/ocaml discussion #14451](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/discussions/14451) on **cross-compilation status** was active: [@yotambarnoy](https://github.com/yotambarnoy) asked about the maturity of cross-compiling OCaml for Android using `make crossopt`, coinciding with the cross-compilation build fixes in [ocaml/ocaml#14835](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14835) and [ocaml/ocaml#14808](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14808).
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Sustained ephemeron correctness effort**: The merging of [ocaml/ocaml#14733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14733) and [ocaml/ocaml#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734) continues a multi-week series of GC fixes led by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), with three more PRs ([ocaml/ocaml#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813), [ocaml/ocaml#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774), [ocaml/ocaml#14745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14745)) in the review pipeline, reflecting the complexity of multicore ephemeron semantics.
- **Targeted, benchmark-backed compiler performance improvements**: [ocaml/ocaml#14832](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14832) (profiling overhead), [ocaml/ocaml#14571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14571) (minor GC), and [ocaml/ocaml#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796) (GC pacing) all follow a pattern of measurable, data-driven optimisation with explicit benchmark results.
- **Cross-compilation robustness**: Multiple PRs ([ocaml/ocaml#14835](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14835), [ocaml/ocaml#14833](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14833), [ocaml/ocaml#14808](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14808)) address cross-compilation edge cases, and active user discussion ([ocaml/ocaml discussion #14451](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/discussions/14451)) signals growing interest in Android and musl targets.
- **OxCaml upstream flow**: Several PRs this week (e.g. [ocaml/ocaml#14832](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14832) from oxcaml/oxcaml#6141, [ocaml/ocaml#14571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14571)) continue the pattern of performance and correctness work originating in the OxCaml fork and flowing back into upstream OCaml.

group_summaries.brief_summary: eio gains io_uring performance flags and fd security fix; ocaml-uring v2.14.0 released with Obj.magic elimination.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: io_uring DEFER_TASKRUN/SINGLE_ISSUER flags boost HTTP throughput ~100k req/s; MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC fixes fd security; AT_FDCWD moved to C.
group_summaries.new_features:
- `eio_linux` now uses **`IORING_SETUP_DEFER_TASKRUN`** and **`IORING_SETUP_SINGLE_ISSUER`** when creating io_uring rings, improving HTTP benchmark throughput by roughly 100k req/s on supported hardware (~350k to ~450k); falls back gracefully on older kernels (pre-Linux 6.1) via `EINVAL` catch ([ocaml-multicore/eio#841](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/841), [ocaml-multicore/eio#842](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/842))
- `eio_posix` now passes **`MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC`** to `recvmsg` when receiving file descriptors, ensuring received fds are close-on-exec atomically; falls back to `Unix.set_close_on_exec` on macOS where the flag is absent ([ocaml-multicore/eio#843](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/843))
- **`AT_FDCWD` handling moved to C stubs** in ocaml-uring, eliminating `Obj.magic` in the OCaml layer; `openat2`, `linkat`, `unlink`, `mkdirat`, and `statx` now accept `Unix.file_descr option` where `None` maps to `AT_FDCWD` natively in C ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/128))
- Fixed **`dio_offset_align` statx getter** in ocaml-uring that was incorrectly returning `stx_dio_mem_align` instead of `stx_dio_offset_align` ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#141](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/141))
- **ocaml-uring v2.14.0** released by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), documenting breaking changes (`Uring.Res` module), new ops (`send_zc`, `sendmsg_zc`), and accumulated bug fixes ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#140](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/140))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was concentrated in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) and [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring), with lighter maintenance work in [ocaml-multicore/meio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio); the remaining six repositories had no recorded activity
- [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) merged four PRs: two io_uring performance flag PRs ([ocaml-multicore/eio#841](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/841), [ocaml-multicore/eio#842](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/842)), one fd security fix ([ocaml-multicore/eio#843](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/843)), and a benchmark Dockerfile update ([ocaml-multicore/eio#844](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/844))
- [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring) shipped the v2.14.0 release ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#140](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/140)), merged the `AT_FDCWD` C-stub migration ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/128)) and a `statx` getter fix ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#141](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/141)), and opened several new PRs for additional ops and bug fixes
- [ocaml-multicore/meio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio) had one new PR opened for a cmdliner migration ([ocaml-multicore/meio#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75)) and three stale PRs closed ([ocaml-multicore/meio#67](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/67), [ocaml-multicore/meio#71](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/71), [ocaml-multicore/meio#74](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/74))
- Contributors active this week: [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris), and [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)
group_summaries.activity_summary: Ongoing PRs for eio chown and incremental directory reading under review; multiple ocaml-uring bug-fix PRs opened but not yet merged.
group_summaries.activity:
- **eio — ongoing:** [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris)'s `chown` support PR ([ocaml-multicore/eio#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781)) continues under review by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), with discussion around making `uid`/`gid` optional and documenting the `follow` parameter
- **eio — ongoing:** [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris)'s draft PR for incremental directory reading ([ocaml-multicore/eio#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821)) received detailed feedback from [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) covering API naming, error handling scope, batching of thread jobs, and resource cleanup
- **eio — new issue:** [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) opened [ocaml-multicore/eio#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845) to track disabling fixed buffers by default in `eio_linux`, after a ZFS kernel bug was reported requiring a reboot when fixed buffers are used; this followed closure of [ocaml-multicore/eio#797](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/797)
- **eio — housekeeping:** Benchmark Dockerfiles updated to reference a recent version of ocaml-uring and work around a change in `opam install --deps-only` behaviour ([ocaml-multicore/eio#844](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/844))
- **ocaml-uring — new ops PR:** [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) opened [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147) adding `shutdown`, `socket`, `renameat`, and `symlinkat` operations with tests (not yet merged)
- **ocaml-uring — bug fixes opened:** `Statx.Attr` inverted-condition bug ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142)), `Region.init` bounds check ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#145](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/145)), `io_uring_submit` error reporting ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/144)), and debug message format fixes ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#146](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/146)) — all opened this week, merging shortly after
- **ocaml-uring — CI:** `lintcstubs-arity` dependency changed from optional to hard test dependency so CI reliably catches missing primitive regenerations ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#143](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/143))
- **meio — backlog cleanup:** [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) closed three stale PRs superseded by the new cmdliner approach: Eio tracing events port ([ocaml-multicore/meio#71](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/71)), OCaml 5.1.0 compatibility generalisation ([ocaml-multicore/meio#67](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/67)), and earlier `Stdlib.Arg` usage error improvement ([ocaml-multicore/meio#74](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/74))
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- The io_uring performance flags merged in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) ([ocaml-multicore/eio#841](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/841), [ocaml-multicore/eio#842](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/842)) depend directly on features available in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring); the benchmark Dockerfile update ([ocaml-multicore/eio#844](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/844)) explicitly pins to a recent ocaml-uring version to enable these flags
- The ocaml-uring v2.14.0 release ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#140](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/140)) consolidated `send_zc`, `sendmsg_zc`, and the `Uring.Res` module changes that eio relies on for its zero-copy and result-handling paths
- `IORING_SETUP_SINGLE_ISSUER` merged in [ocaml-multicore/eio#841](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/841) has a direct interaction with the fork-safety issue tracked in [ocaml-multicore/eio#801](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/801): [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) noted that child processes attempting to use the parent's ring will now receive `EEXIST`, providing a clearer failure mode
- The OCaml 5.x-only question raised in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/144) — prompted by `caml_unix_error` unavailability on OCaml 4.x — is directly motivated by [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) targeting OCaml 5.x exclusively, suggesting a potential future alignment of minimum version requirements between the two libraries
- [ocaml-multicore/meio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio)'s closure of the Eio tracing events port PR ([ocaml-multicore/meio#71](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/71)) reflects the dependency on eio's evolving tracing API, with the stale port from 2024 no longer applicable to the current eio interface
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- In [ocaml-multicore/eio#843](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/843), [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) discussed macOS compatibility for `MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC`: [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) initially flagged that the flag would not work on macOS, while [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) clarified it is POSIX-specified but optional for implementations; the solution uses compile-time detection with a `set_close_on_exec` fallback on macOS
- In [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142), [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) proposed that the `Statx.Attr.check` function should use the abstract `t` type rather than raw `int64`, aligning it with `Attr.mem`; [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) implemented a revised version making the type abstract and adding `Flags.(=)` and `of/to_int` conversions; [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) was consulted on the proposed changes
- In [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/144), [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) raised that `caml_unix_error` is unavailable on OCaml 4.x and proposed dropping OCaml 4.x support for the library, given that its primary consumer eio targets OCaml 5.x only
- [ocaml-multicore/eio#797](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/797) (io_uring security and stability concerns) was closed by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) after triaging the ZFS fixed-buffer kernel crash into the dedicated tracking issue [ocaml-multicore/eio#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845)
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Targeted io_uring flag adoption with benchmarked justification:** three consecutive PRs ([ocaml-multicore/eio#840](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/840), [ocaml-multicore/eio#841](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/841), [ocaml-multicore/eio#842](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/842)) explored `IORING_SETUP_SINGLE_ISSUER`, `IORING_SETUP_COOP_TASKRUN`, and `IORING_SETUP_DEFER_TASKRUN` with careful benchmarking and graceful fallback paths for older kernels
- **Elimination of unsafe OCaml patterns:** the merger of [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/128) removes the last `Obj.magic` usage from ocaml-uring by moving `AT_FDCWD` handling into C stubs; the ongoing `Statx.Attr` abstraction ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142)) continues this push toward type-safe C interop
- **Safer defaults over marginal performance:** the new issue [ocaml-multicore/eio#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845) signals a shift away from fixed buffers as a default in `eio_linux`, preferring stability over the performance benefit after a ZFS kernel crash was reported
- **Cross-platform correctness investment:** both the `MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC` fix ([ocaml-multicore/eio#843](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/843)) and the ongoing `chown` PR ([ocaml-multicore/eio#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781)) required platform-specific handling between Linux and macOS, reflecting sustained attention to portability alongside Linux-specific performance work
- **OCaml 5.x-only alignment:** the question raised in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/144) about dropping OCaml 4.x support from ocaml-uring, driven by eio's 5.x requirement, suggests the lower layers of the stack may follow eio's lead in formalising a 5.x minimum

weekly_summary.brief_summary: Dune 3.24.0~alpha0 and ocaml-uring v2.14.0 released; eio gains ~100k req/s via io_uring flags; ephemeron GC fixes and profiling overhead eliminated in OCaml.
weekly_summary.new_features_summary: io_uring DEFER_TASKRUN/SINGLE_ISSUER boost eio throughput, caml_region C API added to OxCaml, Promise/Fetch/Abort APIs land in js_of_ocaml, and DragonFly BSD support in dune.
weekly_summary.new_features:
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Compiler profiling overhead eliminated** when `-dprofile`/`-dcounters`/`-dtimings` are not in use, removing up to 30% overhead in microbenchmarks with many small functions by skipping `Profile.record_call` unless profiling is requested ([ocaml/ocaml#14832](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14832))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Windows `Unix.select` file-descriptor drop fixed**: `socket_poll_add` returned the dispatched node rather than the updated list head, silently dropping fds and causing a memory leak; a regression test was added ([ocaml/ocaml#14764](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14764))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Two ephemeron GC correctness fixes merged**: [ocaml/ocaml#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734) fixes orphaning during `Phase_sweep_ephe`; [ocaml/ocaml#14733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14733) corrects per-domain ephemeron work counters and removes a pessimistic `ephe_next_round` call causing unnecessary repeated work
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`Char.escaped` documentation corrected** in `stdlib/char.mli`; previous text incorrectly stated double-quote characters are escaped ([ocaml/ocaml#14836](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14836))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ New **`caml_region_begin` / `caml_region_end` C API** added by [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan), providing explicit region management hooks for the C runtime ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6142](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6142))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`[@@flatten_floats]`** made an optional annotation rather than automatic for mixed `float`/`float64` records by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa), giving explicit control over memory representation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6106](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6106))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ Records with **`[@atomic]` fields** no longer receive unboxed versions by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa), preventing unsound interactions between atomicity and unboxed representations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6124](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6124))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`--enable-keyword-edition`** configure option added for the 5.4 branch by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27), allowing builds to opt-in to keyword edition support ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6140](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6140))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`quick_stat`** runtime function optimized by [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) to reduce GC statistics overhead ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6139](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6139))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Merlin synced to `5.2.0minus-39`** by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski), pulling in updated `jkind` scannable constant, `cmi_format` using `persistent_signature`, and `compilation_unit` name mangling overrides ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6136](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6136))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ `eio_linux` now uses **`IORING_SETUP_DEFER_TASKRUN`** and **`IORING_SETUP_SINGLE_ISSUER`** when creating io_uring rings, improving HTTP benchmark throughput by ~100k req/s on supported hardware; falls back gracefully on older kernels via `EINVAL` catch ([ocaml-multicore/eio#841](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/841), [ocaml-multicore/eio#842](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/842))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ `eio_posix` now passes **`MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC`** to `recvmsg` when receiving file descriptors, ensuring received fds are close-on-exec atomically; falls back to `Unix.set_close_on_exec` on macOS ([ocaml-multicore/eio#843](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/843))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **`AT_FDCWD` handling moved to C stubs** in ocaml-uring, eliminating `Obj.magic` in the OCaml layer; `openat2`, `linkat`, `unlink`, `mkdirat`, and `statx` now accept `Unix.file_descr option` where `None` maps to `AT_FDCWD` in C ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/128))

- __RUMINANT:core__ **CI improvements**: [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) disabled "cold" tests in GitHub Actions and AppVeyor CI ([ocaml/ocaml#14821](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14821)); [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) hardened the C++ test to skip on musl libc targets ([ocaml/ocaml#14827](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14827))

- __RUMINANT:core__ **Ongoing — GC pacing**: [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) continues [ocaml/ocaml#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796), with [Eduardo Souza](https://github.com/udesou) providing macro-benchmark results (5×5 parameter sweep across 22 benchmarks) showing wall-time improvements across all tested configurations
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Ongoing — further ephemeron correctness**: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) has three additional ephemeron PRs in review: [ocaml/ocaml#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813), [ocaml/ocaml#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774), and [ocaml/ocaml#14745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14745)

- __RUMINANT:core__ **Ongoing — constructor eta-expansion**: [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14803](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14803) proposing auto eta-expansion of constructors in function-type contexts (e.g. `List.map Some [1;2;3]`)

weekly_summary.cross_repository_work:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Dune 3.24.0~alpha0** appeared simultaneously in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) as a release and in [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) as a draft pre-release PR ([ocaml/opam-repository#29969](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29969)), while the release page was scraped to [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3666](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3666)) documenting the `%{pkg:...}` pform and promoted directory targets
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **eio and ocaml-uring** coordinated closely: io_uring performance flags in eio ([ocaml-multicore/eio#841](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/841), [ocaml-multicore/eio#842](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/842)) depend on features in ocaml-uring; the benchmark Dockerfile update ([ocaml-multicore/eio#844](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/844)) explicitly pins to a recent ocaml-uring version; ocaml-uring v2.14.0 was simultaneously released to [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29954))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune's pure-OCaml patch engine** ([ocaml/dune#14177](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14177)) created cascading effects on [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository): it caused [oxcaml/opam-repository#48](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/48) (resolved) and [oxcaml/opam-repository#51](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/51) (opened), both stemming from stricter line-number matching in the new implementation
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Merlin sync** from [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) into [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) landed via [oxcaml/oxcaml#6136](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6136); separately, [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) had a feature redirect when [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) self-closed [ocaml/merlin#2074](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2074) to open [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1606](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1606) in the LSP server instead
- __RUMINANT:core__ __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **OxCaml-to-upstream flow** continued: [ocaml/ocaml#14832](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14832) (profiling overhead elimination) originated from [oxcaml/oxcaml#6141](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6141); [ocaml/ocaml#14571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14571) (faster minor GC) follows the same pattern of performance work originating in OxCaml flowing back upstream
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Merlin and dune Emacs tooling**: [Danny Willems](https://github.com/dannywillems) opened Emacs compatibility fixes in both [ocaml/merlin#2076](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2076) (Emacs 31 `when-let` compatibility) and [ocaml/dune#14798](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14798) (Emacs byte-compile warnings + CI), coordinating Emacs improvements across repositories
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **OCaml 5.5 compatibility** drove activity across repositories: opam-repository updated constraints for caqti-lwt ([ocaml/opam-repository#29937](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29937)) and camlp5-buildscripts ([ocaml/opam-repository#29950](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29950)); ocaml.org published the OCaml 5.5.0~beta1 changelog ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641))

weekly_summary.notable_discussions:
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ The **jext.0.1.0 syntax error incident** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29957](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29957)) revealed that a malformed opam file caused opam-mirror (a MirageOS unikernel) to crash with a fatal `Stdlib.Parsing.Parse_error`, directly motivating [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid)'s CI fix in [ocaml/opam-repository#29964](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29964) adding `opam lint` to Windows GHA CI
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ In [ocaml-multicore/eio#843](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/843), [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) debated macOS compatibility for `MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC`; [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) clarified the flag is POSIX-specified but optional, with compile-time detection and `set_close_on_exec` fallback on macOS as the solution
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ In [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/144), [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) raised that `caml_unix_error` is unavailable on OCaml 4.x and proposed dropping OCaml 4.x support from ocaml-uring, given eio targets OCaml 5.x exclusively
- __RUMINANT:tools__ On [ocaml/dune#14767](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14767), [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) reported an intermittent missing `.d` dependency file issue and a cache poisoning problem producing null-byte module names after the ocamldep-to-anonymous-actions migration
- __RUMINANT:tools__ The **3.24.0 release tracker** [ocaml/dune#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14620) discussion surfaced an `enabled_if` with `%{read:...}` regression in dune language 3.23 ([ocaml/dune#14789](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14789)) and a `git rev-parse` sandbox-breaking issue that prompted [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) to open a `%{git-sha}` rule variable PR [ocaml/dune#14810](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14810)

weekly_summary.emerging_trends:
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Sustained ephemeron correctness effort**: merging of [ocaml/ocaml#14733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14733) and [ocaml/ocaml#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734) continues a multi-week series of GC fixes by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), with three more PRs ([ocaml/ocaml#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813), [ocaml/ocaml#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774), [ocaml/ocaml#14745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14745)) in the review pipeline
- __RUMINANT:core__ __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Benchmark-backed compiler performance pattern**: [ocaml/ocaml#14832](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14832) (profiling overhead), [ocaml/ocaml#14571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14571) (minor GC), and [ocaml/ocaml#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796) (GC pacing) all follow a pattern of data-driven optimisation with explicit benchmark results, originating in OxCaml and flowing upstream
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Unboxed record representation correctness** receiving focused attention across multiple PRs tightening rules: `[@atomic]` fields ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6124](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6124)), mixed float records ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6106](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6106)), and `any`-kinded fields ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6143](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6143), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6145](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6145), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6156](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6156))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **CFG-level compiler optimizations** growing with both jump threading ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6104](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6104)) and identical code folding ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6148](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6148)) in flight simultaneously
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **Targeted io_uring flag adoption with benchmarked justification**: three consecutive eio PRs ([ocaml-multicore/eio#840](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/840), [ocaml-multicore/eio#841](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/841), [ocaml-multicore/eio#842](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/842)) explored `SINGLE_ISSUER`, `COOP_TASKRUN`, and `DEFER_TASKRUN` with careful benchmarking and graceful fallback paths for older kernels
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **Elimination of unsafe OCaml patterns**: merger of [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/128) removes the last `Obj.magic` usage from ocaml-uring; the `Statx.Attr` abstraction ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142)) continues this push toward type-safe C interop
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **OCaml 5.x-only alignment**: the question raised in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/144) about dropping OCaml 4.x support from ocaml-uring, driven by eio's 5.x requirement, suggests the lower layers of the stack may formalize a 5.x minimum

- __RUMINANT:tools__ **OxCaml feature parity in odoc**: concurrent PRs ([ocaml/odoc#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420), [ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)) target modalities and zero-alloc annotations, continuing the OxCaml toolchain integration pattern across dune, odoc, merlin, and ocaml-lsp visible since week 19
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Eio and OCaml 5+ backend adoption** continues: `irky 0.1` ([ocaml/opam-repository#29925](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29925)), `awso 0.9.1` with Eio backend ([ocaml/opam-repository#29967](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29967)), and `uring 2.14.0` ([ocaml/opam-repository#29954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29954)) all reflect the growing Eio ecosystem
